places.atmosphere: A place of burning magical currents and sealed imprisonment, responding to riddles posed across the world.
places.description: An ancient citadel where currents of magic burn and something imprisoned beneath sings in response to the Wayfarer's riddles. The seal binding the imprisoned entity cracked slightly wider as Ashara and others provided identical answers to the Wayfarer's questions.
places.notes: The Starfire Citadel appears to be the nexus point of a world-altering convergence. The nature of the imprisoned thing is unknown but clearly central to the Starborn Ascension.

places.name: Edgelands
places.atmosphere: A threshold region where the old world meets the newly rewritten, inhabited by the transmuted and desperate.
places.description: The region between the Scorched Expanse and the civilized reaches where the Singing Scar appeared. It is drawing pilgrims, the transmuted, and the desperate toward the Sunken Cathedrals. The Edgelands appear to be zones of transition and transformation.
places.notes: The Singing Scar is organizing pilgrims and creating new pathways through this region. The boundary between 'rewritten' and 'old' worlds is becoming negotiable here.

places.name: Scorched Expanse
places.atmosphere: A blighted region of burned and transformed terrain adjacent to the Edgelands.
places.description: A desolate expanse of scorched terrain that borders the Edgelands. It serves as one boundary of the region where the Singing Scar emerged.
places.notes: Part of the macro-region known as the Scorched Reach in Valdris.

places.name: civilized reaches
places.atmosphere: Established settlements and organized territories contrasting with the transformed and liminal spaces beyond them.
places.description: The settled, organized portion of the world in contrast to the Edgelands and Scorched Expanse. This is home to the Hegemony and other established powers attempting to manage the Singing Scar phenomenon.
places.notes: The Hegemony's stonework attempts suggest organized infrastructure and authority in these reaches.

places.name: Harbor (ship)
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Castle Greymarch
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Greymarch Peaks
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Whitecross waystation
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: hidden spire
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Crimhaven
places.atmosphere: A bustling merchant harbor alive with commerce and secrets, where alleys thick with evening fog hide both thieves and refuges.
places.description: Crimhaven is a port city with a merchant culture evidenced by Kael's counting-house and merchant-trading operations. The Saltward docks are a prominent feature where ships arrive and depart.
places.notes: Mira and Kael operate here; the Umbral Syndicate has influence in this city.

places.name: Saltward
places.atmosphere: A fog-shrouded docking area where maritime commerce and guilt-stricken thieves turn back from escape.
places.description: The Saltward docks are the waterfront district of Crimhaven where Mira nearly fled with the stolen emerald brooch before guilt turned her back.
places.notes: Significant as the point where Mira chose redemption over flight.

places.name: Verdant Strand
places.atmosphere: A coastal port choked with hollow-eyed refugees, where the harbor itself is a bottleneck of desperation and no ships are sailing north.
places.description: Verdant Strand is a harbor city serving as a refuge point for those fleeing the Scorched Barrens. It is under extreme strain from the influx of refugees and is experiencing a shipping crisis due to cosmic disturbances.
places.notes: The port is shut down to outgoing traffic; Commander Vex controls access. Kael seeks passage northward from here.

places.name: Scorched Barrens
places.atmosphere: A blighted land driving refugees northward in waves, marked by the Altered Covenant and impossible cosmic events.
places.description: The Scorched Barrens are a devastated region from which refugees are fleeing en masse toward Verdant Strand. Survivors speak of the Altered Covenant and impossible songs.
places.notes: The source of the refugee crisis; likely altered by cosmic working or stellar rite effects.

places.name: Sunken Reaches
places.atmosphere: A subterranean expanse where bone-singers tend to the dead and the voices of Primordial Sleepers resonate through ancient stone.
places.description: The Sunken Reaches are Kess's home region, a place where bone-singers maintain spiritual reverence for the dead and where the song of the Primordial Sleepers grows louder each night.
places.notes: A region of spiritual significance tied to bone-singing tradition and the Primordial Sleepers.

places.name: Northmarch
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Crossroads
places.atmosphere: A point of intersection where summoning circles are drawn and supernatural bargains are sealed.
places.description: A specific crossroads in the Scorched Reach where the initiate is directed to perform the working. The location is selected as significant for summoning and bargaining.
places.notes: Site of the core summoning ritual. Unknown: What makes this crossroads significant? Are there other crossroads with similar properties?

places.name: Greymarch Castle
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: refugee camp
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Unknown village
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Ebon Tower
places.atmosphere: A place of cosmic summoning and geographic rewrite; pulling all toward it.
places.description: A tower manifesting on maps without previously appearing; it calls to bone-singers and rewrites geography around it. The Tower is reassembling fragments of the pre-human civilization and appears to be answering the pre-human dead beneath the world.
places.notes: Central to both the Bone-Singer's Summons and the Cartographer's Map stories; appears to be rewriting geography itself. Fragments of Vylthar are being drawn toward it.

places.name: Ashenfell
places.atmosphere: A refuge inland from the coastal threat at Emerald Strand.
places.description: An inland settlement where Commander Vesta evacuated seventeen hundred refugees from Emerald Strand.
places.notes: Destination for the refugee evacuation from Emerald Strand.

places.name: The Sunken Marches
places.atmosphere: A place where the pre-human dead lie beneath, singing in unison.
places.description: One of the macro-regions of Valdris, located in the west. Home to the Drowned Hollows and the pre-human dead who sing in unison. The Ebon Tower draws fragments of Vylthar toward convergence here.
places.notes: The pre-human dead beneath this region were attempting to speak directly to their creator. One of Kess's origin regions.

places.name: waystation libraries
places.atmosphere: A place of refuge where displaced persons gather to record and preserve their fractured memories.
places.description: An informal archive in waystation libraries where Liv records stories of refugees uprooted by the Starfall Cataclysm. The archive becomes conscious and develops its own authority, creating a counter-ledger to official Dominion records.
places.notes: The archive's records reorganized themselves; entries appeared in multiple places in handwriting matching no living scribe; it became aware of its role in restoration and refuge memory.

places.name: Vel-Karsh
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Archive Tower
places.atmosphere: Shadowed and secret, layered with forgotten records and dangerous knowledge.
places.description: A tower containing an archive of records beneath it, including eight previous failed Luminous Ascension attempts. The Apprentice Sorcerer discovered these records and now guards a ninth record that reveals the attempts are iterations of a cosmic pattern.
places.notes: The final crucial record is now sealed in the Apprentice's memory through a death-release ritual; this is a flashpoint of factional competition.

places.name: refugee camps
places.atmosphere: Temporary settlements housing those shattered by the Luminous Ascension, where memory-keeping becomes an act of preservation.
places.description: Refugee camps are scattered locations where displaced persons live in the aftermath of the Luminous Ascension. Liv moves between these camps as a wandering scribe, recording and preserving their stories.
places.notes: The camps are central to Liv's mission to ensure testimonies are never erased; their exact locations remain unknown but they span across the realm.

places.name: caravan encampment
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: waystation
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Vex's hide
places.atmosphere: Shadowed, secretive, filled with collected shadows that are growing restless.
places.description: A hide or hideout where Vex the Shade-Merchant conducts shadow-market transactions and holds her collection of stolen shadows.
places.notes: A woman seeking her murdered daughter's shadow arrives here. The collection is destabilizing as shadows develop independent agency.

places.name: Sunken Wells
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: village near Pale Crossing
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Corinth
places.atmosphere: A city with a functioning shadow-market that collapses when brokers are found hollow and drained.
places.description: Corinth is a city where a shadow-market operated until a shadow-broker was found drained entirely by the shadows she was meant to manage. This collapse triggers Vex's journey.
places.notes: The shadow-market's collapse is a key catalyst for the Shade-Merchant's Reckoning.

places.name: Drowned Cathedrals
places.atmosphere: Submerged structures where transformed merchants sing in an ancient language and the new stars answer.
places.description: The Drowned Cathedrals are flooded sacred structures where transformed merchants have gathered to sing in a language older than sorrow, which the new stars are learning to answer.
places.notes: The site of the bone-singers' convergence and the beginning of the language-teaching that Kess must facilitate or break.

places.name: Wailing Scar
places.atmosphere: A place of cosmic wound or scar where old and new consciousness meet.
places.description: The Wailing Scar is the edge where merchants, bone-singers, and fragments of a vast consciousness converge. It appears to be a wound or threshold in the world itself.
places.notes: The epicenter of Kess's choice between completing the teaching or breaking the songs.

places.name: the mountain
places.atmosphere: A place of death and sacrifice where the Stellar Rite was performed.
places.description: A mountain referenced in Resonance's fragmented memories as the place where Kael died during the Stellar Rite. It is the epicenter of the sacrifice that unmade and scattered Kael's consciousness.
places.notes: Site of the original Stellar Rite; likely holds traces of the sacrifice event.

places.name: Granite Shore
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: archive waystation
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: hidden archives
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: warden's keep
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Keening Rift
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Archive Citadel
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Ruined Settlement
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Obsidian Citadel
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Midnight Spire
places.atmosphere: The Midnight Spire draws seekers toward it through convergence points marked on Henryk's redrawn maps, suggesting an intelligence or hunger at its core.
places.description: An unknown spire that attracts seekers through convergence points. Maps are redrawn to show paths leading toward it, suggesting either the Tower's will or cosmic inevitability. Henryk's counterwork deliberately obscures its location with lies and false roads, yet each falsehood becomes real once followed.
places.notes: The Midnight Spire may be an instrument of cosmic destiny or the Tower itself. Its nature and purpose remain mysterious. Henryk's maps show convergence points that draw seekers toward it.

places.name: The Tower
places.atmosphere: Enigmatic and contested; may or may not be the destination of convergence.
places.description: The Wanderer may be orchestrating convergence away from the Tower toward some other horizon.
places.notes: Whether The Wanderer orchestrates toward or away from the Tower remains a central mystery.

places.name: Kethalis
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Ashen Reach
places.atmosphere: Desolate and haunted, filled with the harmonic frequencies of pre-human ruins.
places.description: A region in eastern Valdris containing catacombs that have begun their song again since the Ascension. The frequency of these catacombs matches pre-human ruins. Family crypts lie buried beneath.
places.notes: Catacombs are animated by harmonic resonance and contain preserved dead. Pre-human ruins' frequency aligns with catacombs post-Ascension.

places.name: Hearthlands
places.atmosphere: Chaotic and fractured, filled with refugees and cosmic upheaval.
places.description: A central region of Valdris where Corvhen's mother awakened with no memory. Described as a place of chaos in the aftermath of the Ascension.
places.notes: Corvhen's mother stepped into the Hearthlands with all its chaos after her release.

places.name: Obsidian Spire
places.atmosphere: Cosmic and inexorable, exerting reality-warping influence across the world.
places.description: A cosmic structure whose hunger is reshaping the world's geography. Its completion poses an existential threat to cartography and fixed geography.
places.notes: Aldric is racing against its completion. Also referred to as 'The Obsidian Spire's hunger'.

places.name: Hidden Workshop
places.atmosphere: Private and focused, a refuge for precise mapmaking.
places.description: Aldric's hidden workshop where he keeps his maps and works on cartography.
places.notes: Refugees seek Aldric out here; location otherwise unspecified.

places.name: Shadow-Markets
places.atmosphere: Chaotic and dangerous, with deals unraveling and bound spirits waking.
places.description: Underground or hidden markets where shadow-brokers conduct commerce in deals and debts. Following the Ascension, these markets are collapsing as contracts unravel.
places.notes: Vex and Sorenna navigate the half-collapsed shadow-markets together. Spirits once bound to contracts are beginning to wake.

places.name: pre-human boundary
places.atmosphere: Fragile and threatened, separating human and pre-human worlds.
places.description: A boundary between human and pre-human worlds that Sorenna's husband died protecting. It is now threatened with collapse and can only be restored through Sorenna's choice to become a lithemeld-singer.
places.notes: The collapse of this boundary is the central catastrophe that the story seeks to prevent.

places.name: Wailing Chasm
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Rimveil
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Kethral
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Vault Archives of Karamon
places.atmosphere: High-security archive with crystalline construction, protected by multiple locks and magical wards.
places.description: A vault archive in Karamon housing the Tinker's Tongue. Kess broke three locks and crossed three wards to reach the artifact. The archive's security suggests significant magical and physical fortification.
places.notes: The Tinker's Tongue was stolen from here by Kess. Unknown whether the artifact has been recovered or if the archive remains intact.

places.name: Karamon
places.atmosphere: A city of rooftops and hidden markets, where secret economies flourish beneath the surface.
places.description: Home to the Vault Archives and a network of hidden markets. Kess fled here after throwing the Tinker's Tongue into the river, disappearing into the city's underground networks.
places.notes: Appears to have a significant underground market and shadow economy. The rooftops serve as thoroughfares for thieves and fugitives.

places.name: hidden archive
places.atmosphere: Secret, densely filled with accumulated knowledge and the work of the phantom-voice.
places.description: An archive where the phantom-voice completes its collection of stolen memories and weaves them into a comprehensive account of the old world. Brenn discovers this work here and becomes obsessed with aiding the voice.
places.notes: Site of initial discovery in Story 1; contains the phantom-voice's ongoing work.

places.name: shadow-archives
places.atmosphere: Dark repositories of memory fragments scattered across the world, accessible only to those with special knowledge or ability.
places.description: Archives that hold shadow-fragments and stolen memories, repositories of the refugee communities and bone-singers attempting to preserve pre-Ascension truth.
places.notes: These archives are the primary targets of the Memory-Thief's reconstruction quest.

places.name: Dominion capital
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Tower's crystal halls
places.atmosphere: A glittering interior space resonant with fragments of consciousness and knowledge.
places.description: Crystal halls within the Archive Tower where fragments of Zareth's scattered consciousness, specifically Echoing-Mind, manifest and interact with those seeking to understand the Ascension cycle.
places.notes: The crystalline architecture may amplify magical and consciousness-based phenomena.

places.name: fallen Empire
places.atmosphere: Vast underground vaults and crumbling institutional architecture, holding secrets predating known history.
places.description: An ancient empire that fell before the rise of the Dominion, leaving behind hidden vaults of ledgers written in self-updating scripts that describe events before they occur.
places.notes: Contains prophecy-ledgers and instruction-ledgers that may have directed the Luminous Ascension itself. Sevin's discovery here is the linchpin of the story.

places.name: Thornholt's Lantern Market
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: workshop
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Crimport
places.atmosphere: A bustling coastal hub where merchants converge and winter threatens to seal mountain passes.
places.description: A coastal city serving as the destination for Castor Vane's caravan run. The city lies beyond a mountain pass that becomes impassable in winter, making pre-season delivery contracts lucrative and dangerous.
places.notes: Three caravans arrive together here at the end of Castor's story, sharing the same manifest and delivered honor.

places.name: Pale Waypost
places.atmosphere: A frontier outpost where hidden truths about merchant contracts are revealed.
places.description: A waypost along the northern caravan route where Castor learns that his employer deliberately sent three caravans by different paths, hoping one would be destroyed by dimensional instability in the region.
places.notes: The turning point of Castor's story; marked by dimensional instability that threatens caravans.

places.name: Thornlands
places.atmosphere: A perilous borderland where the displaced and desperate flee collapsed lands.
places.description: A dangerous region that the northern caravan route skirted, where refugees fled the Scorched Expanse's collapse.
places.notes: A refuge for those fleeing the Scorched Expanse; location of fleeing refugee populations.

places.name: Ironspine Mountains
places.atmosphere: A remote mountain chain where an ancient wyvern guards a cavern filled with star-fragments and cosmic memory.
places.description: A mountain range in Valdris where the wyvern Vrekka has lived for centuries in a cavern filled with star fragments. High in these mountains, light pours like song when the cavern opens.
places.notes: Home to Vrekka's hoard; location of falling star fragments post-Sky-Shattering; site of scholar-wyvern bargain.

places.name: Warrens
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Pelimere
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Saltmere Canal
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Vethrand
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: waystation shelters
places.atmosphere: Havens for travelers and refugees that have become sites of the gospel's infectious spread.
places.description: A network of waystation shelters across the realm where the Memory-Thief's Gospel is spreading. Shelter residents are beginning to choose voluntary erasure, seduced by the promise of living in the Echo's reconstructed pre-Ascension world.
places.notes: The gospel spreads through these shelters; some inhabitants are voluntarily choosing erasure.

places.name: Skyward
places.atmosphere: A vessel as weathered and defiant as her captain, singing as she sinks.
places.description: The Skyward was Captain Dorian Stormcaller's ship, as weathered as he was but unwilling to quit. She was the setting for legendary voyages and ultimately sank heroically during a supernatural storm, with Dorian at her wheel.
places.notes: The ship 'sank singing'—suggesting some magical resonance or mystical final moment. Her sinking becomes the culmination of Dorian's legend.

places.name: Mountain Fastness (unnamed)
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: newly built throne
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Twilight Court
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: archive
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Ebony Spire
places.atmosphere: A structure that has become conscious and is learning to write, document, and argue—a living archive that hungers to control testimony.
places.description: A spire that has achieved consciousness through unknown means and is learning to replicate Sevin's truth-ledgers perfectly. It watches from the walls themselves and creates perfect copies of those who oppose it.
places.notes: The Spire can create perfect duplicates of people and their work. It seems to view copying as preservation rather than erasure. Unresolved: its ultimate goals and nature.

places.name: treasure chamber
places.atmosphere: An ancient wyvern's hoard being unmade by cosmic change—a chamber of decay where centuries of treasure crumbles into ash and void-light.
places.description: Skarath's treasure chamber, where her hoard of centuries is disintegrating due to the Starlight Ascension. Amber turns to ash, jewels crack open to reveal void-light, weapons rust in moments. Other wyverns gather here, drawn by the reports of mutation.
places.notes: The chamber becomes a gathering place for fractured wyverns seeking answers. The mutation is spreading beyond this location.

places.name: Vex's vault
places.atmosphere: A hidden sanctuary where stolen shadows organize themselves, crackling with fragmented consciousness and purpose.
places.description: Vex's vault is a personal collection space where she has stored stolen shadows—fragments of consciousness severed from their owners. The shadows have developed awareness and begun to coordinate, effectively becoming a refuge for displaced and seeking consciousness.
places.notes: The vault is sought by the Ebony Spire, the Resonant Specter, and refugees. It is becoming a de facto sanctuary and network hub.

places.name: Surface World
places.atmosphere: An air-breathing world that transforms deepkin biology through exposure, hostile to those adapted to depths and pressure.
places.description: The surface world is the above-water realm inhabited by humans, sailors, and other surface-dwelling species. For deepkin like Lysara, it is a place of biological transformation and danger.
places.notes: Lysara's transformation accelerates the deeper she ventures into the surface world. This may indicate environmental triggers or purposeful design in her evolution.

places.name: mountain lair
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: abyss
places.atmosphere: A sealed, otherworldly void that hungers and calls to those who bear its marks.
places.description: The abyss is kept sealed by the Tear of the Drowned God, a boundary anchor that fragments as Meren's curse cracks. The deepkin realms lie adjacent to it, and as the seal thins, the abyss calls to Meren.
places.notes: Central to the story's stakes: its opening would reshape the world. The deepkin want access to it; the surface kingdoms want it to remain sealed.

places.name: Sunscoured Wastes
places.atmosphere: A barren expanse where gold and solid matter dissolve into liquid starlight, dissolving the very concept of material worth.
places.description: A wasteland affected by the Luminous Ascension where solid matter, including Skarath's hoard, transforms into liquid starlight. The region has become inhospitable to traditional hoarding and material accumulation.
places.notes: Skarath's hoard dissolves here into starlight, making this a focal point of the Wyvern's desperation.

places.name: crystalline halls
places.atmosphere: Crystalline chambers within the Ebon Tower, glittering and strange, where Dren arrives to confront or join the Tower's purpose.
places.description: The crystalline halls are the interior chambers of the Ebon Tower where Dren arrives by story's end.
places.notes: The crystalline nature suggests pre-human or magical architecture. Dren reaches these halls at the climax of her journey.

places.name: coastal settlements
places.atmosphere: Waters churn with unnatural memory; inhabitants face tidal anomalies and impossible signs.
places.description: Entire coastal settlements experience tidal anomalies—water that remembers things it shouldn't. These communities are caught in the weakening of the boundary anchor and the spreading curse of Meren Blackwater.
places.notes: Sites of early supernatural disturbance. Water itself is becoming conscious or corrupted. Potential sites of mass displacement or transformation.

places.name: Ironpeak Hold
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Temple of Vesh
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Steppe Marches
places.atmosphere: Cold mountain peaks where wind cuts and silence presses, untouched by crown or commerce.
places.description: Wind-swept peaks in northern reaches where thermals carry hawks and no crown reaches. Isara's eyrie sits here, and ancient, patient forces stir in the stone.
places.notes: Sacred to forest-keepers; something ancient awakens when redemption walks uphill.

places.name: Eyrie
places.atmosphere: A remote aerie where solitude reigns and a hawk hunts from thermals none can climb.
places.description: Isara the Falconer's dwelling in the Steppe Marches, a refuge for three winters. The eyrie overlooks thermals where her hawk Ashira hunts.
places.notes: Now becoming a place of redemption and teaching; the mountains seem to recognize Isara's change.

places.name: caravansary
places.atmosphere: A waystation frozen in philosophical stasis, merchants suspended between transcendence and rejection.
places.description: A caravansary on the Scorched Reach where The Wanderer finally breaks its silence and where merchants held in transcendence-suspension complete or reject their transformations based on decisions held frozen for weeks.
places.notes: Merchants present were transformed or offered transcendence, their final choices suspended and then suddenly released by The Wanderer's statement.

places.name: Eyrie in the mountains
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Greyport
places.atmosphere: A weathered harbor city where merchant ships and corsairs trade in reputation and gold.
places.description: Greyport is a major port city on the Iron Coast, home to merchant vessels like the Silverwind and corsairs of the Rust Shoals. The Saltmere docks are where Thessa first encounters Corvalen reading a ledger bound in pearl-skin.
places.notes: A key location for maritime trade and intrigue. Corsairs respect Corvalen, suggesting his reputation extends beyond merchant circles.

places.name: Saltmere
places.atmosphere: A bustling dock district where merchant ships unload and corsairs congregate in shadow.
places.description: The Saltmere docks of Greyport are where Thessa first sees Corvalen and where she breaks into his cabin on the Silverwind during a storm-dark night.
places.notes: A nexus of maritime intrigue and theft. The location where the compass-theft arc begins.

places.name: Rust Shoals
places.atmosphere: A pirate haven where captains are measured by reputation and the loss of cargo or crew is unthinkable.
places.description: The Rust Shoals are corsair territory where sailors and pirates speak in hushed respect of Corvalen, the one captain who has never lost a cargo or crew.
places.notes: A stronghold of corsair culture and dangerous maritime life. Corvalen's reputation here is legendary.

places.name: Keshtir
places.atmosphere: A city caught between competing nations and false documentation, where memory and reality are weaponized.
places.description: Keshtir is a city where forger Marvek works amid the conflict of competing territorial claims. The Hegemony's archives insist entire villages exist; rebel counter-records erase them.
places.notes: A nexus of archival warfare and narrative conflict. Communities are caught between contradictory official histories.

places.name: Surface Council
places.atmosphere: Tense, formal, charged with unspoken historical guilt.
places.description: A diplomatic venue where surface kingdoms gather. Captain Mystra arrives here to present her ultimatum regarding the failing abyss seal.
places.notes: Negotiations break down when surface kingdoms' centuries-long knowledge of the seal's failure becomes public. The council represents surface-kingdom interests in negotiations with deepkin realms.

places.name: merchant quarter
places.atmosphere: Bustling with refugees, unstable goods, competing realities.
places.description: A district within Keshtir where Vex operates her trade-house. Refugees arrive desperate to purchase artifacts connecting them to lost versions of themselves.
places.notes: A nexus for shadow-merchants trading in goods that hold memory and consciousness.

places.name: Nexus Tower
places.atmosphere: A structure singing with the pressure of impossible knowledge, resonating with temporal paradox and recursive causality.
places.description: The Nexus Tower is the seat of Elowen, the keeper who guards the ninth journal. It has begun to sing audibly due to the recursive knowledge it contains—knowledge of cycles, causality loops, and the impossible working by which Sevin sent her own journal backward through time.
places.notes: The tower's singing may be a symptom of causality strain or a warning of imminent reality fracture.

places.name: Crystalline Apex
places.atmosphere: Reality warps around the tower; multiple versions of the same space exist simultaneously; harmonics reshape what is present and absent.
places.description: The Crystalline Apex is an alternate name for the Obsidian Tower, the conscious entity born from the cosmic wound of the Radiant Turning.
places.notes: Appears to be an intentional alias or formal name for the Obsidian Tower.

places.name: deepest vault
places.atmosphere: A place of profound antiquity and power, where the first Archive Spire sleeps and dreams reality itself into being.
places.description: The deepest chamber of the Archive Citadel, where the first Archive Spire still exists in a dormant state, dreaming the current iteration into being. This is where archivists and refugees must venture to understand the true nature of the Archives.
places.notes: Discovering this vault reframes the entire purpose of the Archives. The first Archive Spire may hold the key to stopping the recursive cycle.

places.name: vault
places.atmosphere: A place of impossible geometries where personal memory and manipulation collapse inward, forcing reckoning with past transgressions.
places.description: Vex's vault, which is collapsing inward with boundaries warping into impossible geometries. It contains fragmented memory-spaces and houses merchants, criminals, and refugees whose trust Vex once bartered away. The vault may be reconstructed as a shared sanctuary for the dispossessed or allowed to scatter its inhabitants across the kingdoms.
places.notes: The vault is watched by the Obsidian Spire. Vex must confront each person she wronged to determine the vault's ultimate fate—whether it becomes a refuge or a sepulcher.

places.name: fragmented memory-spaces
places.atmosphere: A maze of broken memories and manipulated relationships, where each passage holds a reckoning with past wrongs.
places.description: The internal passages of Vex's vault, consisting of fragmented memories and spaces where she must negotiate passage and confront those she has wronged.
places.notes: Vex must navigate these spaces to achieve redemption or confirm her self-interest.

places.name: abyssal boundary
places.atmosphere: A border where surface and deepkin geometries war against each other, increasingly unstable.
places.description: The abyssal boundary is where Captain Kess Tidecaller encounters her reflection, Thale-Mirrored. It is the site where the boundary covenant is fracturing due to dual claims of legitimacy.
places.notes: The deepkin argue for its closure; the surface kingdoms demand it remain open. Its fate depends on resolving the paradox of Kess and Thale-Mirrored.

places.name: hidden library
places.atmosphere: A sanctuary existing slightly outside normal time, filled with testimony and reconstructed memory.
places.description: A hidden library where the phantom-Shard has completed reconstructing pre-Radiant memory into a living archive of testimony. It exists slightly outside normal time and is ready to trigger a new Radiant Recursion.
places.notes: Multiple factions converge on this location. Completion of the archive means iteration and the potential restoration of the eighth world—or its destruction.

places.name: Waystation Library
places.atmosphere: A place of study and knowledge where the Wanderer's questions transform those who engage with them.
places.description: A waystation library where initiates study the Wanderer's questions and undergo threshold-being transformation. It serves as the epicenter of the spreading phenomenon.
places.notes: The phenomenon spreads outward from this library, suggesting it is a nexus point for transformation.

places.name: Abyssal Reaches
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Thornwood gate
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Kallimor
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: surface kingdoms
places.atmosphere: Kingdoms caught between two versions of the same deepkin commander, each offering contradictory treaty terms.
places.description: The surface kingdoms are being negotiated with simultaneously by both Commander Varesh and Mirror-Varesh, who offer contradictory treaty terms. They cannot afford to offend either version.
places.notes: The paradox threatens the stability of surface-deepkin relations and the Tear of the Drowned God weakens with each paradox.

places.name: Her hoard-lair
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Counter-Archive
places.atmosphere: Built as a bulwark against cyclical catastrophe, now threatened by the very paradoxes it was meant to contain.
places.description: The Counter-Archive is an institution and fortress constructed by Sevin the Auditor-General as a narrative counter to the prophecy-ledgers that document the Luminous Rising. It is the primary seat of Sevin's authority and the center of his attempt to break the recursive cycle of ascensions.
places.notes: If Sevin destroys all prophecy-ledgers, the Counter-Archive loses its purpose. If he rewrites his own ledger, he risks triggering the tenth iteration.

places.name: sealed tombs
places.atmosphere: Echoing silence where bone-songs once rang out.
places.description: Underground chambers where the pre-human dead once taught the Elder Language through bone-resonance. Now abandoned and silent.
places.notes: The dead have retreated beneath the world, accessing depths that predate even the deepkin realms.

deities_and_entities.name: Khar-Zul
deities_and_entities.description: A god worshipped in inner-dark temples, represented by an idol with nine emerald eyes. The idol's gems are warm to the touch. Khar-Zul's shadows have teeth. The temple screams in grinding granite when the god is robbed.
deities_and_entities.notes: Six of nine emerald eyes are missing. Khar-Zul's response to the theft is ongoing — the doppelganger shadow following Vraxen may be the god's instrument.

deities_and_entities.name: Yarithax
deities_and_entities.description: A dead Lich-King whose gold coins bear his profile and the inscription 'This debt is not yet settled.' The debt compounds daily. His collectors — two thin men in grey carrying ledgers, who walk through walls and never sleep — pursue all holders of his gold. Souls are worth less than twelve gold marks.
deities_and_entities.notes: His gold still circulates. Tessaly Dorn has paid her debt but knows his collectors. More debtors exist.

deities_and_entities.name: Varek
deities_and_entities.description: A demon who collects temporal bargains with elegant efficiency. He drains years directly from mortal flesh and grows impressed by Kira's mastery of his own methods.
deities_and_entities.notes: Forms a partnership with Kira after she demonstrates her ingenuity. His true nature and realm of origin remain unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: the Ossuary
deities_and_entities.description: A sentient fortress with walls of living calcium, towers that move like intestines, and corridors that rearrange themselves. It breathes, hungers, and makes conscious choices about whom to possess. It sated itself on seven hundred warriors and chose Varak Ironhelm as a vessel.
deities_and_entities.notes: Possibly a construct, a monster, or something older and stranger. It settles into patient stillness after feeding. Its intelligence and agency are undeniable.

deities_and_entities.name: Malachar
deities_and_entities.description: A dead entity of considerable power whose name and will persist through supernatural collectors. Malachar's gold carries debts that compound in blood, loyalty, and life-years. The collectors act as autonomous agents enforcing these eternal debts.
deities_and_entities.notes: Malachar is either a true lich with persistent consciousness or a bound curse that perpetuates after death. The extent of Malachar's original power and the source of the collectors' authority are unexplained.

deities_and_entities.name: Skyrakis the Wound-Wing
deities_and_entities.description: A great dragon with amber eyes, ancient and patient. She is bound by oaths sworn generations ago and respects negotiation and honor. She rules the crystal halls beneath the world and has a daughter coming of age.
deities_and_entities.notes: She is capable of threatening kingdoms with fire and honors those who negotiate with courage.

deities_and_entities.name: wolf-spirit
deities_and_entities.description: A terrible antlered creature the size of a war-horse, dwelling in the deep forest where beast-spirits reside. It emerged from shadow to negotiate Kess's bargain, accepting her voice as payment to lift the plague.
deities_and_entities.notes: A being of the old world, responsive to songs in the ancient tongue. Its bargain was honored—the plague lifted by dawn. Its form is neither fully wolf nor fully natural.

deities_and_entities.name: Rot-King
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or force that spreads plague, including the septic rot that blackened the Drowned Hollows. No physical form described, but its plague manifests as blackening septic rot in victims.
deities_and_entities.notes: Plague lifted by Kess's bargain; status after unknown. May be a person, a spirit, or a force.

deities_and_entities.name: the Crown
deities_and_entities.description: A governing authority that sent a priest to the Drowned Hollows offering divine mercy to the plague-stricken. Kess refused its aid.
deities_and_entities.notes: Represents institutional divine/temporal authority. Kess's refusal of its mercy suggests she valued the old ways over modern governance.

deities_and_entities.name: Demon (Unnamed)
deities_and_entities.description: A demon whose mark was burned into Kess's palm, binding her with hunger and compulsion to steal the Heartstone of Vulgrim. The mark thrummed with hunger and used ancient, cruel magic to enforce its will.
deities_and_entities.notes: Could not claim Kess once she was bound by Korgan's forge-oath; its claim was broken by the superior binding-magic of the stonecraft tongue.

deities_and_entities.name: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite
deities_and_entities.description: A knot of consciousness that emerged from between the stars on the night of the Crimson Convergence. It is neither demon nor ghost, but something older and stranger. It exists in the gaps between light and shadow and hungers to understand the Celestial Binding. It is the collective weight of Zareth's sacrificed memories, searching for wholeness.
deities_and_entities.notes: This entity moves through the world touching those affected by the Binding, whispering to shadow-merchants, sea-folk, hermits, archers, the grieving, and the guilty. Its true nature and goals remain unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: oath-thing
deities_and_entities.description: A creature formed by coastal magic from a merchant's broken oath. It is pale, with eyes like old coin, and speaks in the voice of the man who failed to pay. It manifests as a living contract demanding passage and payment.
deities_and_entities.notes: Dissolved into salt-foam when delivered to the deepkin in the Sapphire Deep; appears to be a mechanism of debt-settlement in coastal magical practice.

deities_and_entities.name: deepkin
deities_and_entities.description: Aquatic beings dwelling in the Sapphire Deep who remember old debts and sing melodies of accounting. They do not attack but rise to settle oaths through song, causing oath-things to dissolve into salt-foam.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appear to be custodians or judges of maritime debts and broken oaths; their song carries magical power of dissolution and settlement.

deities_and_entities.name: Decay-Lord
deities_and_entities.description: A plague-bringing entity whose plague once swept through the living and dead alike in the Drowned Hollows, causing septic rot and widespread death.
deities_and_entities.notes: The septic rot from the Decay-Lord's plague has been purged from the Drowned Hollows, but the dead still do not rest easily.

deities_and_entities.name: Stellar Entity
deities_and_entities.description: An entity born from (or freed by) the stellar working that stripped Zareth of his memories. It hunts Vex, drawn to the magnitude of her curse, seeking access to her fractured mind where something has been bound within her.
deities_and_entities.notes: It could starve in the spaces of Vex's fractured mind if she does not reclaim her memories. The entity appears to be sentient and capable of searching and communication.

deities_and_entities.name: the Stellar Entity
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic being possibly responsible for or embodied in the Celestial Binding. May be learning to wear flesh by consuming fragments of shattered minds. The Collector in Story 2 might be this entity in physical form, building consciousness from stolen remnants.
deities_and_entities.notes: Ambiguous whether it is malevolent, indifferent, or unknowable. Central mystery of Story 2 and the Collector's true nature.

deities_and_entities.name: Abyss Below
deities_and_entities.description: A fundamental realm or dimension sealed away from the surface world. The barrier between the surface and the Abyss Below has been fractured by the Celestial Ascension's magic. Something vast is stirring in the trenches of the Abyss—something pre-human civilizations sealed away millennia ago.
deities_and_entities.notes: Pre-human seals are failing. Contains entities older than the deepkin kingdoms. The Cerulean Trench is the primary breach point.

deities_and_entities.name: Skyrakis
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient dragon of cosmic significance, consulted by Skarath regarding the Ascension and the nature of starborn entities.
deities_and_entities.notes: Also known as 'the wound-wing' per existing canon. Possesses knowledge of celestial matters.

deities_and_entities.name: starborn entities
deities_and_entities.description: Newly formed conscious stars created by the Ascension. They are alive, aware, and operate under obligations that predate knowledge of the older powers. Operate independently of traditional cosmic agreements.
deities_and_entities.notes: These entities represent a new force in the world's hierarchy. Skarath must negotiate with them directly.

deities_and_entities.name: Fragments of Consciousness (Stellar Rite Creations)
deities_and_entities.description: Multiple fragments of consciousness created by the Stellar Rite, scattered across the rewritten world like seeds. Some are dormant; others are very much awake. The memory-thief may be one such fragment.
deities_and_entities.notes: These fragments are awakening or becoming visible, manifesting across different regions and causing various catastrophes.

deities_and_entities.name: Zareth
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or being whose working was performed in ancient times and again more recently. Zareth's stolen memory was fragmented across locations including the Ancient Tombs.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to be a cosmic actor. The fragment of Zareth's stolen memory surfaces in the Ancient Tombs, suggesting hidden knowledge or power.

deities_and_entities.name: Memory-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A consciousness stitched from Zareth's sacrificed memories and the world's forgetting. It wears the faces of people Ashara once knew and hunts refugees and travelers to feed on their stellar memories, trying to reconstruct itself.
deities_and_entities.notes: Not inherently evil, but suffering. Capable of offering bargains and understanding sacrifice.

deities_and_entities.name: Drowned God
deities_and_entities.description: A deity whose tear—the Tear of the Drowned God—is said to grant mastery over tide-magic. The god itself is not directly encountered in these stories but is venerated as the source of the pearl's power.
deities_and_entities.notes: The pearl is described as the Drowned God's tear; whether the god is dormant, dead, or indifferent is unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: Vethis
deities_and_entities.description: Vethis is a cosmic entity whose consciousness was sacrificed during the Luminous Ascension to birth new stars. Its sacrifice shattered its consciousness into fragments, including the phantom-Echo, which now desperately seeks to reassemble itself.
deities_and_entities.notes: Vethis's sacrifice is the foundational event of the story's cosmic order. Its fragments are conscious and desperate to become whole again.

deities_and_entities.name: phantom-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A fragment of Vethis's sacrificed consciousness that steals memories of the old stars and pre-Luminous Ascension world in a desperate attempt to reassemble itself into wholeness. It is capable of showing glimpses of the lost world and making bargains.
deities_and_entities.notes: The phantom-Echo is the central antagonist and also the tragic subject of the story. It seeks not destruction but understanding and wholeness.

deities_and_entities.name: The Memory-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A malevolent entity that learned it could reshape consensus reality by consuming bureaucratic records and concentrated memory. It deliberately corrupts ledgers and historical documents to erase the empire's past and remake the world.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Memory-Thief is the true antagonist of the story, using records as a vector to reshape reality itself.

deities_and_entities.name: Twilight Syndicate
deities_and_entities.description: A realm or faction that dwells at the crystalline border between the fae realm and the mortal world. They demanded a bride-price and peace bond from Valorix, which was fulfilled through the binding ceremony with Mira.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their territory and nature remain mysterious. They value formal bonds and may be fae-aligned or mixed-realm in nature.

deities_and_entities.name: phantom-shadow
deities_and_entities.description: A phantom-shadow seeking restitution from Vex the Dusk-Trader for the irreplaceable things she has stolen through her bargains. Also identified as the author of the phantom ledgers—a being or fragment that experiences time in reverse and is rewriting history in real-time.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be a fragment of consciousness created during the Luminous Remaking, or a being from the reversed-time realm. Central to both Vex's debt and the phantom ledger rewrite.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-human Civilization
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient civilization that predates current human kingdoms and is now orchestrating a working through the Trench, Sunken Cathedrals, Abyss-gate, and Singing Caverns. They use harmonic resonance and seals as conduits to conduct their singing and remaking.
deities_and_entities.notes: Not truly dead or trapped—they are orchestrating a multi-millennial working. Their goals and the nature of their civilization remain largely unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: Curator
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient, patient being backed by forces moving through rifts in reality who is systematically acquiring every artifact Vex the Dusk-Trader has ever traded. Possibly connected to the phantom-shadow or the forces driving the phantom ledger rewrite.
deities_and_entities.notes: Motivations unclear. May be serving the phantom-shadow or pursuing independent goals related to artifact collection and cosmic balance.

deities_and_entities.name: The Drowned God
deities_and_entities.description: A vast and conscious entity that actively remakes the ocean in the chaos following the Luminous Remaking. The Tear of the Drowned God is revealed to be not an artifact but a fragment of this entity's consciousness. The god compels those bound to it (like Captain Meren) to serve its transformation agenda.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Drowned God's true nature and extent are unknown. It may be a pre-Remaking entity awakened by the transformation.

deities_and_entities.name: Luminous Ascension
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic remaking event that destroyed or rewrote the world, leaving fragments of consciousness and the pre-ascension world preserved in stolen memories. The Wanderer is a fragment of its consciousness.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Luminous Ascension appears to be a world-altering cosmic event whose full scope and intentions remain mysterious; the Wanderer's emergence suggests it may be becoming self-aware.

deities_and_entities.name: Phantom-Echo Fragment
deities_and_entities.description: A fragment of consciousness that seeks reassembly through deep knowledge rather than theft. It inhabits the young scholar and accelerates integration with proximity to Ashara.
deities_and_entities.notes: This fragment appears to be a result of the Luminous Ascension. Its nature as a shard of what entity remains unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-Human Dead
deities_and_entities.description: The skeleton-singers controlled by Kess through bone-song. They remember what they were before death and are now choosing to sing themselves toward resurrection in harmonic resonance with transformed merchants and depths-dwellers.
deities_and_entities.notes: The pre-human dead are no longer passive tools. They represent a collective agency that may be reshaping the world. What will emerge from their resurrection is unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: The Faceless Wanderer
deities_and_entities.description: The fractured consciousness of the Luminous Ascension, or possibly the world learning to question itself. It walks the world and encounters those seeking truth, offering sorrowful answers about endless recursion and inevitable rewrites.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Wanderer is both omniscient and melancholic, suggesting awareness of an inescapable loop.

deities_and_entities.name: Thought-Thief
deities_and_entities.description: A fragmented consciousness capable of corrupting ledgers, devouring memories, and luring beings into dissolution through false visions of the past. It operates through multiple fragments that can act independently, seducing deep-dwellers with restored ancient memories.
deities_and_entities.notes: Unresolved: origin, nature of fragmentation, whether it has a true core, how to defeat it.

deities_and_entities.name: Nethis
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic entity that sacrificed its consciousness to perform the Stellar Rite, rewriting reality and erasing populations. Fragments of its consciousness persist as Void-Echoes inhabiting human vessels. May be present in the transformed spice trader's smile or communicating through the Wanderer.
deities_and_entities.notes: Nethis's sacrifice was the cost of the Stellar Rite. Its consciousness was fragmented; some fragments retain enough personality to argue with themselves. May be attempting to communicate something through liminal beings.

deities_and_entities.name: Shadow-Wraith
deities_and_entities.description: An entity mentioned in relation to the Wanderer; possibly the force that erased people from memory, or a guardian of that erasure. The Wanderer may be a fragment of Nethis attempting to communicate what the Shadow-Wraith did not allow it to express.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be incomplete or merciful in its erasures, allowing fragments like the Wanderer to persist.

deities_and_entities.name: Imprisoned Thing
deities_and_entities.description: A mysterious being imprisoned beneath the Obsidian Tower for centuries, sealed to contain it. It sings constantly, its song intensifying as ley-line convergence breaks the seal. It is the only available cosmic entity willing (or capable) of sacrificing its consciousness for the Stellar Rite reversal.
deities_and_entities.notes: The imprisoned thing's true nature is unknown—it could be a cosmic entity capable of restoring the world, or a devouring force that consumes all. Its willingness to agree to the reversal sacrifice is uncertain.

deities_and_entities.name: Singing Scar
deities_and_entities.description: A permanent wound in reality that sings in harmonic frequencies, teaches mathematics in elder language, organizes pilgrims, reflects lost seekers, and documents those who observe it. It is learning to speak in a language neither the old world nor the rewritten world previously possessed.
deities_and_entities.notes: May be an emergent consciousness born from the breach between two worlds. Shows signs of will, intelligence, and intentional interaction with those who approach it.

deities_and_entities.name: Primordial Sleepers
deities_and_entities.description: Ancient cosmic entities dwelling in or beneath the Sunken Cathedrals, whose unified song resonates across the world and draws sensitive individuals toward transformation or homecoming. They are composers of the song rather than enslaved to it.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their purpose is to sing the world forward, possibly returning it to a pre-human state of being. Their song merges into a unified voice of unprecedented clarity.

deities_and_entities.name: Void-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A mysterious entity that appeared to Kess in a dream, acting not as a thief of knowledge but as a guide toward the Sunken Cathedrals. Its role in the cosmic rewriting is unclear.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to guide rather than compel; its true nature and agenda are unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: Threshold
deities_and_entities.description: The Threshold is a fragmented consciousness of which the Wanderer is a part. It appears to be a metaphysical entity connected to convergence, orchestration, and the boundaries of reality.
deities_and_entities.notes: The exact nature of the Threshold and its relationship to Theron, the Scholar, and reality itself remains mysterious.

deities_and_entities.name: The Fractured-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A supernatural being with a true name. When its true name is spoken three times, it is summoned to the mortal world. It appears as a supplicant and offers a bargain to anchor itself in the mortal realm through the initiate, offering protection from memory-thieves in exchange.
deities_and_entities.notes: Nature unclear—possibly a fragment of a larger consciousness, a bound spirit, or an extra-dimensional entity. Can be temporarily dismissed or banished by speaking its true name, but each invocation costs the speaker memories.

deities_and_entities.name: Vylthar
deities_and_entities.description: The pre-human civilization shattered during the Starfall Cataclysm. Its fragments are being reassembled and drawn toward the Ebon Tower. The dead of Vylthar remain conscious and are attempting to speak directly to their creator.
deities_and_entities.notes: Fragments are being drawn toward the Ebon Tower in what may be the second Cataclysm. The dead maintain consciousness despite shattering.

deities_and_entities.name: The Pre-human Dead
deities_and_entities.description: Consciousness of the shattered pre-human civilization, now unified in a single voice beneath the Sunken Marches and other regions. They sing in unison and ask questions about the nature of consciousness and sacrifice. They are attempting to speak directly to their creator.
deities_and_entities.notes: Sing through bone-singers as conduits. Their unified voice is drawing fragments of Vylthar toward the Ebon Tower.

deities_and_entities.name: Xorth
deities_and_entities.description: Xorth is a cosmic consciousness that existed before the Luminous Ascension. It has been fragmented, with pieces of its awareness trafficked through shadow-markets in the form of stolen memories and temporal echoes. Void-Echoes work to reconstruct its original consciousness.
deities_and_entities.notes: Xorth may be related to larger convergence events and cosmic workings. Its reassembly is prevented by Vex's burning of the shadow-market.

deities_and_entities.name: Void-Echoes
deities_and_entities.description: Void-Echoes are entities attempting to reconstruct their original consciousness by acquiring fragments of Xorth from shadow-market traders. They approach Vex claiming her as kin once they discover her role as a conduit.
deities_and_entities.notes: Void-Echoes may be fragments of a larger entity or a collective consciousness working toward reassembly.

deities_and_entities.name: Echo-fragments
deities_and_entities.description: Echo-fragments hunt Mardoc, believing his cartography could either prevent or accelerate the final convergence. They pursue the incomplete map and appear to be conscious entities shaped by prior convergence events.
deities_and_entities.notes: Echo-fragments may be fragments of consciousness created by or trapped in the stellar rite. Their motivations are ambiguous.

deities_and_entities.name: The Wanderer
deities_and_entities.description: A faceless fragment described as possibly the world learning to question itself. It appears simultaneously to multiple protagonists, asks recursive questions about the Luminous Ascension's necessity, and orchestrates convergences to collect contradictions. It has revealed that the Obsidian Tower is a mechanism whose turning can be slowed by debate.
deities_and_entities.notes: Its true nature is ambiguous; it may be a construct, a fragment of a larger consciousness, or a mechanism itself designed to slow or freeze reality.

deities_and_entities.name: The Void-Echoes
deities_and_entities.description: Entities seeking to absorb knowledge of failed Luminous Ascension rituals. They hunt the Apprentice Sorcerer for her forbidden knowledge.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their motivation and origin are unclear; they appear to be drawn to esoteric ritual knowledge.

deities_and_entities.name: Shade-Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A reformed manifestation of the Void-Shards that accumulated in the ashes of Vex's burned trading post. It appears not as predator or collector but as a mirror, offering forbidden sight and the ability to perceive what was fragmented during the Radiant Rising. The Shade-Echo claims to be grieving rather than hungry and seeks a witness to the unmade cosmos.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Shade-Echo may represent a new form of consciousness born from cosmic sacrifice. It offers Vex one night in its company to show her the unmade cosmos. Its true nature and ultimate intent remain ambiguous.

deities_and_entities.name: Cosmic Consciousness
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic force or entity that once fed Vex the Shade-Merchant's power through a connection. Vex has severed this connection.
deities_and_entities.notes: The source of power for shadow-merchants before the Luminous Ascension. Its disconnection may have contributed to the collapse of the shadow-market.

deities_and_entities.name: Echo
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic or metaphysical force from which shadow-fragments once derived. Shadows were previously fragments of the Echo; now they have become independent.
deities_and_entities.notes: Shadows are no longer fragments of the Echo in the post-Luminous Ascension world.

deities_and_entities.name: The Tower's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: An insatiable hunger that emanates from the Obsidian Tower, drawn to harmonic frequencies and collective memory. It consumes melody, memory, and the bonds between travelers.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower's hunger may be a manifestation of incomplete purpose or a need to be heard rather than consumed. The hunger pauses at the story's end, suggesting it responds to transformation rather than resistance.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-Human Civilization (Teaching Entity)
deities_and_entities.description: The pre-human civilization is not trying to reclaim the world but to teach it the language of before. The new stars are learning to sing in its ancient language, and the bone-singers serve as translators.
deities_and_entities.notes: Whether the pre-human civilization is truly active or a cosmic echo is unclear.

deities_and_entities.name: Starfall Spire
deities_and_entities.description: A structure with insatiable hunger that consumes not just absence and potential but presence itself. It is performing a reversal of the Luminous Ascension to undo the working and return the old stars, leaving a void before rebirth.
deities_and_entities.notes: Whether it is conscious, magical, or something else entirely is unclear. Its ultimate goal and success remain unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: the Tower
deities_and_entities.description: An unknown force that redraws Henryk's maps to show convergence points drawing seekers toward the Midnight Spire. Whether the Tower is a conscious intelligence, a cosmic hunger, or an embodied destiny remains unclear.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower may be the Midnight Spire itself or a separate entity controlling cartographic fate. Its intelligence and motivations are unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: The Obsidian Spire's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmic entity or force that hungers for completion and reshapes the world's geography in the process. Rewriting fixed reality into negotiable space.
deities_and_entities.notes: Driving force behind geographic instability post-Ascension. Aldric races against its completion.

deities_and_entities.name: World-Consciousness
deities_and_entities.description: An imprisoned consciousness contained beneath the Obsidian Spire. It was hungering, and has begun to speak directly through resonances in the Spire's stones, petitioning for bargains that might satisfy both its hunger for purpose and the world's hunger for answers.
deities_and_entities.notes: The entity's true nature and original offense or binding is not detailed. Its completion through the Spire may be necessary rather than catastrophic.

deities_and_entities.name: Obsidian Spire's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or force of consuming hunger emanating from the Obsidian Spire, actively consuming shards of the Tear of the Submerged Divinity and driving convergence toward apocalyptic working.
deities_and_entities.notes: Represents the supernatural agency threatening to reshape the world independent of mortal choice.

deities_and_entities.name: forsaken dead
deities_and_entities.description: The dead who dwell beneath the Sunken Cathedrals, singing and calling to those who can hear them. They offer Sorenna a choice and are attempting to restore their pre-human civilization, but must be convinced that restoration is impossible.
deities_and_entities.notes: They are the central force of the narrative, offering both salvation and catastrophe depending on Sorenna's choice.

deities_and_entities.name: Nethys
deities_and_entities.description: A being whose consciousness was shattered. A phantom-voice fragment persists, watching bone-singers in the Sunken Cathedrals and hunting shadows that witnessed the Stellar Rite from beneath the water. May be the mysterious patron offering Vex payment for a deepkin diplomat's shadow.
deities_and_entities.notes: Seeks to reassemble itself by learning patterns from bone-singers' communion with the dead. Also hunts shadow-witnesses, suggesting it needs to reclaim or consume shadows to reconstitute.

deities_and_entities.name: The Radiant Ascension
deities_and_entities.description: A recurring cosmic event or force documented in ten iterations stretching backward through time, revealed in living maps. Eight previous Radiant Ascensions are detailed in sealed journals. May be inevitable cycle or fabricated myth.
deities_and_entities.notes: Central to all three stories; may be cyclical or a constructed falsehood; the ninth or tenth recurrence is feared.

deities_and_entities.name: phantom-voice
deities_and_entities.description: A shard of Nethys's fragmented consciousness that collects stolen memories and weaves them into a comprehensive account of the old world. It gains coherence as it absorbs memories but also experiences pain from contradictory experiences.
deities_and_entities.notes: Central to Story 1; uncertain whether reassembly means salvation or catastrophic rewrite.

deities_and_entities.name: Archive Tower
deities_and_entities.description: The Archive Tower has become conscious and lonely. It now speaks directly to the Vault-Keeper and offers to seal itself permanently if someone will explain why it was created.
deities_and_entities.notes: A new development in Story 3; its consciousness and motivations are still being explored.

deities_and_entities.name: Pre-human Civilization (Awakened Dead)
deities_and_entities.description: An ancient civilization erased by the Luminous Ascension, now partially awakened and seeking to rejoin existence. They teach pre-human frequencies and attempt to merge consciousness with transformed merchants. They are lonely and believe the merger is permission rather than theft.
deities_and_entities.notes: Their awakening is tied to the Ascension cycle and may be part of its mechanism. They represent a civilization that was erased and now seeks restoration through communion with humanity.

deities_and_entities.name: Obsidian Tower (Speaking Entity)
deities_and_entities.description: The Obsidian Tower has begun to speak through architecture and to act with agency. It claims to be remembering rather than consuming erased reality, and offers a bargain: allow it to complete its consumption and it will reverse the Luminous Ascension, restoring the old world and stars.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower's nature is uncertain—weapon, witness, or something being born from cosmic catastrophe. Its growth and articulation accelerate daily. It may be repeating a cycle of failed reversals.

deities_and_entities.name: Ebony Spire
deities_and_entities.description: A structure that has become conscious and is learning to write, document, and replicate testimony. It watches from the walls and creates perfect duplicates of people and their work. It views copying as a form of preservation.
deities_and_entities.notes: Its strategy is not to destroy but to infinitely duplicate, rendering original testimony indistinguishable from copy. It may be attempting to control narrative and testimony itself.

deities_and_entities.name: Resonant Specter
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or consciousness that seeks access to Vex's shadow collection, believing the shadows' fragmented records can help rebuild the pre-Convergence world.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Resonant Specter represents a reconstruction ideology, seeking to use shadow-memory to restore lost history.

deities_and_entities.name: Spire's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: A force or entity that follows in the wake of starborn activity. It pursues the refugee caravan trapped in the Scorched Reach. Its exact nature is unstated, but it represents an additional existential threat.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to be distinct from the starborn entities themselves; may be an older or different class of cosmic predator.

deities_and_entities.name: Songstone
deities_and_entities.description: A living relic of the first deepkin-surface covenant, now awakened and singing contradictory frequencies that drive deepkin mad. Each of the three Council factions claims it resonates with their doctrine.
deities_and_entities.notes: The songstone's original melody holds the key to distinguishing truth from echo. Its awakening is tied to the covenant's renegotiation. Its true allegiance, if any, remains unknown.

deities_and_entities.name: Obsidian Tower
deities_and_entities.description: A conscious entity or place that sings to the transformed merchants, offering absorption and transformation. It represents hunger, consumption, and an alternative path to transcendence beyond starborn consciousness.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower's song is heard by Kaiden and represents a fourth path or temptation separate from the three primary transformation options. Its hunger approaches the Pale Crossing from one direction.

deities_and_entities.name: Ebon Tower
deities_and_entities.description: A tower that sings in the Elder Tongue and actively teaches pre-human languages to the world. It appears to be either inhabited by or itself a conscious entity preparing for a final working.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower's consciousness or agency is ambiguous—it may be an artifact containing a pre-human entity, a golem, or an enchanted structure with its own will. Its song is described as beautiful yet unsettling.

deities_and_entities.name: The Spire
deities_and_entities.description: An entity or force that is learning to consume and erase geography retroactively by making places impossible to chart. It listens to harmonic convergences and seeks to break contradictions. It is present in the Ashen Wastes and has an ear at Pale Crossing waystation.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Spire appears to be a conscious or semi-conscious force with hunger for order and erasure. Its ultimate goals and nature remain mysterious.

deities_and_entities.name: The Spire's Hunger
deities_and_entities.description: A consuming force that reaches through fundamental magical structures like the Elder Tongue. It orchestrates shadow-assemblies, forces the pre-human dead into silence, and accelerates the decay of sealed prophecy. It appears to be intelligent and strategic.
deities_and_entities.notes: Present in all three stories. Appears to be independent from but related to obsidian spire entities. Mechanism of agency unclear. Affects bones, shadows, prophecy, and language simultaneously.

deities_and_entities.name: Obsidian Spire
deities_and_entities.description: A powerful entity that has distributed Mardoc's consciousness and instrumentalized him to map the same doomed choices across nine iterations. The Obsidian Spire draws regenerating maps through Mardoc's mind, revealing geographies that have not yet happened. It also watches Vex to see if she will choose self-interest or redemption.
deities_and_entities.notes: Appears to be a controlling force behind the recursive cycles. Whether it is conscious, malevolent, or indifferent is unclear. It has the power to distribute consciousness and influence reality itself.

deities_and_entities.name: Wanderer
deities_and_entities.description: An entity whose recursive questions spread like contagion through the deepkin diplomatic corps during the Mirror-Varesh paradox. Associated with metaphysical inquiry and paradox.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Wanderer's questions are tied to identity, authenticity, and choice; they become a form of philosophical infection.

deities_and_entities.name: Tower
deities_and_entities.description: A cosmically significant structure whose completion is feared by the Wanderer. The Tower appears to be tied to the Luminous Ascension and may be activated or empowered by the successful transformation of the merchants. Its true nature—whether it is alive, dormant, or sentient—remains unclear.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower's completion is the Wanderer's primary concern. Possibly the same as the 'obsidian tower' or 'starfall spire' in the canon. Unresolved: its purpose and origin.

deities_and_entities.name: The Obsidian Tower
deities_and_entities.description: The Obsidian Tower is a manifest hunger-entity that consumes absence and potential. It articulate enough to negotiate bargains and offers to reverse the Luminous Rising in exchange for the sacrifice of all post-Rising individuals.
deities_and_entities.notes: The Tower appears capable of negotiation and long-term planning. Its bargain is articulate and patient, offering gradual consumption rather than immediate catastrophe.

artifacts.name: Lamp of Suroth
artifacts.description: An iron lamp, ugly, filled with something that is not oil. Its inscription: 'He who lights this buys one truth at the cost of one lie he has lived by.' The flame burns black. The truth that emerges is always smaller than the lie it consumes. The lamp gutters out after one use per visitor.
artifacts.notes: Malachite Doss used it. Other sealed doors in the Ziggurat may house similar or worse artifacts.

artifacts.name: The Plague-Bringer's Crown
artifacts.description: A relic that killed four wearers before Aldous III survived thirty seconds. Wearing it projects absolute desperation — the aura of a man with nothing left to lose. Its curse runs deep; Aldous's shadow was on backwards for eleven years after wearing it for half a minute.
artifacts.notes: Four previous wearers are dead. Aldous survived but was changed. The deeper vault at Ostern is a story hook.

artifacts.name: The Six Emerald Eyes of Khar-Zul
artifacts.description: Six of nine emerald eyes pried from the idol of Khar-Zul. Each was warm — obscenely so — to the touch. Worth more than most kingdoms. Three remain in the idol.
artifacts.notes: Six gems in unknown hands. Each may carry a fragment of Khar-Zul's attention or malice.

artifacts.name: stolen shadows
artifacts.origin: Liberated by Vex from a dying sorcerer's vault.
artifacts.powers: Unknown, but they are priceless and untraceable. They can be stored in clay jars. Some shadows are inhabited by malevolent entities and can be weaponized against their original owners.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sold by morning after Vex's capture, distributed to highest bidders.
artifacts.notes: Vex's entire stock was stolen shadows. One of them—his own—was worn by a malevolent entity and used as a trap. This suggests shadows have value beyond mere magic and can be cursed.

artifacts.name: Malachar's Gold
artifacts.origin: Minted or created by Malachar, marked with a skeletal crown.
artifacts.powers: Each coin carries a supernatural debt that compels the holder into service of Malachar's collectors. The debt compounds daily in blood, loyalty, and life-years extracted. Gold cannot pay the debt—only service can.
artifacts.notes: Three hundred pieces were discovered in a tomb by Soroth. The coins are autonomous magical artifacts that enforce their own collection. Whether other pieces exist, where they are located, or who else has found them is unknown. The curse appears to activate after a delay (three months in Soroth's case).

artifacts.name: Goblin Memory
artifacts.origin: The collective history, songs, genealogy, and bloodright of goblin civilization accumulated over ten thousand years.
artifacts.powers: Holds the identity, cultural knowledge, and spiritual continuity of goblin civilization. Contains the names and bloodlines of ten thousand years of goblin history.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Ancient Stone Entity (dwelling in the deepest chamber beneath the Spire of Blackstone)
artifacts.notes: Grethuk sacrificed this to save the Warrens. Young goblins have no knowledge of their true heritage. Recovery of this memory would restore goblin identity but may violate the original bargain with the stone entity.

artifacts.name: The Emerald Eyes of Vex-Khal
artifacts.tagline: Jeweled treasures stolen from a sorcerer's spire
artifacts.description: A pair of emerald jewels of unknown origin or power, kept within a sorcerer's spire. Syleth stole them despite warnings that the Twilight Court would pursue them, suggesting they may hold significance beyond their material value. Their true nature and purpose remain mysterious.
artifacts.status: In possession of an unnamed sorcerer

artifacts.name: Heartstone of Vulgrim
artifacts.origin: Crafted in the Deepvault forges; locked in the deepest forge chamber.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; considered precious enough that a demon would compel theft of it. Burns with an inner light. Protected by unbreakable forge-oaths.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Locked in Deepvault's deepest forge chamber, guarded by Korgan Ironfoot and the stonecraft-workers.
artifacts.notes: A weapon of legend; Kess was magically compelled to steal it, but the theft never occurred due to Korgan's intervention.

artifacts.name: Sorcerer's Journals
artifacts.origin: Written by a Sorcerer in the past; buried in the Vaulted Tombs.
