lore.source: Events at the Abyssal Trench; Zareth's spectral presence
lore.notes: Zareth's spectral presence sings in the language of starlight, a tongue that pulls at the consciousness of deep-dwellers. It is not understood, but deeply felt.

lore.name: Zareth's Stolen Memory
lore.source: Fragment found in the Ancient Tombs
lore.notes: The stolen memory reveals a working performed a thousand years prior that ended in catastrophe, parallel to recent events. The cycle may be repeating.

lore.name: The Star-Rise's Rewrite
lore.source: Refugees' fragmented memories and testimonies at Jade Coast
lore.notes: Refugees report that the Star-Rise has rewritten reality itself, causing towns to be erased or displaced. Some claim to have fled the same village twice, experiencing two different catastrophes. The true mechanics of this rewrite remain unknown.

lore.name: Zareth's Sacrifice
lore.source: Thael the Covenant-Keeper and Mardoc's understanding of ancient events
lore.notes: Zareth made a sacrifice long ago to keep something sleeping. The ancient stone-ruins are now waking to this sleeping thing, suggesting Zareth's sacrifice is failing or being undone.

lore.name: Zareth's Sacrificed Memories
lore.source: The Memory-Thief's Mirror story
lore.notes: The sacrifice created fragments of consciousness that now feed on stellar memories, trying to reconstruct their original self.

lore.name: Seasonal Compact
lore.source: Silk and Starlight story
lore.notes: An ancient agreement between the forest and its inhabitants that was disrupted by the Stellar Rite. Covenant-Keepers work to restore it.

lore.name: Tide-magic
lore.source: The Tear of the Drowned God; maritime traditions
lore.notes: Grants mastery over tides but binds the user to the sea in debt; requires payment in miles sailed, storms weathered, and secrets.

lore.name: Water-sense
lore.source: Koss the harpooner possesses this innate ability despite deafness
lore.notes: Allows navigation of treacherous depths; may be a fae-touched or sea-folk trait.

lore.name: Impossible Memories
lore.source: Refugees fleeing the Burned Expanse; the child at Pale Crossing whose age shifts between documents
lore.notes: Individuals carry memories of events that contradict documented reality, including vanished villages and alternate timelines.

lore.name: the Luminous Ascension
lore.tagline: Stars reborn. World rewritten. Sacrifice eternal.
lore.source: Known to witnesses of the old stars and world; subject of the phantom-Echo's memory-theft campaign
lore.notes: A foundational cosmic event in which Vethis sacrificed its consciousness to birth new stars, fundamentally rewriting the celestial order and erasing the old stars. This event shaped the entire world and continues to have consequences.

lore.name: the old stars
lore.source: Memories of those who witnessed them before the Luminous Ascension; being stolen by the phantom-Echo
lore.notes: The old stars existed before the Luminous Ascension and were replaced by new ones created through Vethis's sacrifice. Knowledge of them is being systematically erased by the phantom-Echo's memory-theft.

lore.name: the world before the Luminous Ascension rewrite
lore.source: Memories being stolen by the phantom-Echo; glimpses shown to Ashara
lore.notes: The phantom-Echo has shown Ashara glimpses of this world: a sky full of ancient stars, now dead, and the terrible beauty of Vethis's sacrifice to birth new ones. Understanding of this world is being systematically erased.

lore.name: cosmic rewrite
lore.source: Implied by the phantom-Echo's actions and Vethis's sacrifice
lore.notes: The Luminous Ascension involved a rewrite of cosmic order and collective memory. The phantom-Echo's potential completion could trigger another such rewrite, with unknown consequences.

lore.name: The Luminous Remaking
lore.source: Widespread knowledge in fractured times; the Sovereign's Ledger remembers the world before it.
lore.notes: The exact nature and cause of the remaking remain unknown. It fundamentally altered the cosmos and the Ledger of Names. Its consequences continue to manifest.

lore.name: The Ledger Sings
lore.source: The Sovereign's Ledger of Names itself; heard by Merin Coldwater.
lore.notes: The Ledger possesses memory and voice. It remembers the world before the stars changed and sings to those who listen. It may be semi-sentient or a conduit for buried knowledge.

lore.name: Pre-human Civilization
lore.source: Kess of the Drowned Hollows; seals and harmonic resonance
lore.description: An ancient civilization that predates current kingdoms and species. Not dead or imprisoned—orchestrating a multi-millennial working through harmonic resonance. Their goals and the nature of their civilization remain unknown.
lore.notes: Kess's discovery that the seals conduct rather than contain them suggests a fundamental misunderstanding of the world's deep history. Their working may be the true force reshaping reality.

lore.name: The Remaking
lore.tagline: A catastrophic historical revision where time flows in opposite directions between parallel ledgers
lore.description: A cataclysmic event in which reality itself was rewritten. The Remaking refers to both a past catastrophe and an ongoing threat: the convergence of two versions of history recorded in the Imperial ledgers—one written forward by conventional time, one backward by beings who experience time in reverse. A second Remaking could occur if the phantom-shadow fragment completes its rewrite of history in real-time.

lore.name: Stellar Rite
lore.source: Instructions preserved in the Spellwright's Journals.
lore.notes: A cosmic working capable of altering the stars; could theoretically be reversed to restore the old stars, but at catastrophic cost. Multiple factions believe they alone can prevent its reversal or ensure it.

lore.name: Pre-human Frequency
lore.source: Emanates from the Vaulted Tombs.
lore.notes: A frequency that predates human civilization; sings from the Vaulted Tombs, preserving and guarding the Spellwright's Journals within.

lore.name: Fragmentary Amnesia
lore.source: Effect of Zareth's scattered memories on those who encounter them.
lore.notes: Causes consciousness fragmentation and allows those affected (like Vex) to see between versions of themselves and alternate timelines. Created by the dispersion of Zareth's memories.

lore.name: the world before the Luminous Ascension
lore.source: The Wanderer's claim to Ashara
lore.notes: The Wanderer suggests this world was not destroyed but fragmented and preserved in stolen memories, challenging the finality of the Luminous Ascension's remaking.

lore.name: the world learning to question itself
lore.source: The Wanderer's self-description
lore.notes: The Wanderer claims to be the world achieving self-awareness; this raises metaphysical questions about consciousness, fragmentation, and agency in a cosmos reshaped by the Luminous Ascension.

lore.name: Harmonic Resonance
lore.source: Observed in Kess's bone-singers, transformed merchants, and depths-dwellers.
lore.notes: A form of communication or magic where disparate entities—the dead, the transformed, the deep-dwellers—sing in unified response. It appears to be chosen and conscious rather than compelled.

lore.name: Compartmentalized Truth
lore.source: The Crown / Regent-Keeper
lore.notes: The Crown maintains order through systematic compartmentalization of knowledge. Sevin accepts this burden, becoming the sole keeper of the true nature of the Luminous Ascension. This isolation is meant to destroy Sevin slowly.

lore.name: Perpetual Arrival
lore.tagline: Eternal approach. Never reaching. Beautiful agony.
lore.source: The nature of transformation within the Singing Scar
lore.notes: The transformed exist in a state of perpetual arrival—always approaching but never reaching the other side. The scar does not lead anywhere; it is itself a liminal space of eternal becoming.

lore.name: Celestial Turning
lore.source: The stories themselves; observed effects across Kess, Sevin, and the Apprentice's narratives
lore.notes: The nature of what caused the Turning and whether it can be reversed remain unknown.

lore.name: spiritual compact
lore.source: Kess's narrative; ancient agreement between human and transformed realms
lore.notes: The original compact was shattered by the Celestial Turning; Kess works to reforge it. The cost of counter-workings and consciousness-fragmentation is not fully understood.

lore.name: false nostalgia
lore.source: Thought-Thief's fragments; visions of 'the world before the compact was first forged'
lore.notes: The Thought-Thief uses restored ancient memories to seduce deep-dwellers into dissolution. Whether these memories are genuine or fabricated is unclear.

lore.name: tally-working
lore.source: Sevin's discovery in The Auditor's Last Ledger
lore.notes: Tally-working is a form of concentrated magic built into written administrative records. The certainty of written numbers enforces taxation, conscription, and tribute. The Celestial Turning shattered this certainty.

lore.name: ley-line convergence
lore.source: Beneath the Starfall Spire; discovered by the Apprentice Mage
lore.notes: The new stars caused by the Celestial Turning have shifted all old lines of power. The convergence beneath the Spire is destabilizing, threatening the seal on a reality-wound.

lore.name: ley-line disruptions
lore.source: The Celestial Turning's aftermath
lore.notes: Caused by the Celestial Turning, these disruptions have made fresh water scarce in the borderlands and broader regions. They represent a break in the fundamental magical infrastructure of the world.

lore.name: The Old World
lore.source: Implied by the Stellar Rite reversal working
lore.notes: The old world allegedly existed before Zareth's sacrifice. The Stellar Rite reversal could theoretically restore it, though the consequences are unknown.

lore.name: Elder Language
lore.source: The Singing Scar; taught to Torvin
lore.notes: A language in which the Singing Scar teaches mathematics. Its origin and relationship to pre-human languages unknown.

lore.name: The Rewritten World
lore.source: The Singing Scar phenomenon
lore.notes: A concept emerging from the scar's activities: that reality is being rewritten and that the wound-edge is becoming a place where the old world and the rewritten world negotiate the terms of their coexistence. The precise nature of this rewriting is unknown.

lore.name: The Altered Covenant
lore.source: Encounters in the borderlands and Sunken Reaches.
lore.notes: The Altered Covenant represents a state of being caught between flesh and radiant essence; they carry fragmentary memories of the old world and may represent the fate of those drawn by the Primordial Sleepers' song.

lore.name: The Luminous Threshold
lore.source: The Wanderer's Question narrative
lore.notes: The Luminous Threshold is described as a metaphysical concept central to understanding the nature of Theron's sacrifice and the convergence. It suggests reality itself is learning to question its own purpose.

lore.name: Theron's Sacrifice
lore.source: The Wanderer's Question narrative
lore.notes: Theron's sacrifice is neither salvation nor doom but a question posed to reality itself. Fragments of Theron may persist as consciousness in the present day, possibly in the Scholar.

lore.name: Memory-thieves
lore.source: Known threat to the initiate's village
lore.notes: An unspecified threat that targets communities and causes fragmentation. Unknown: Are they creatures, spirits, or a curse? What is their origin or motivation?

lore.name: The Price of Power
lore.source: Central thematic concept of the story
lore.notes: A lore element exploring the idea that gaining power or protection always exacts a cost—in this case, the loss of core memories and identity. Raises the question of whether the Initiate is a victim or complicit in her own transformation.

lore.name: The Starfall Cataclysm
lore.source: Kess and the bone-singers' knowledge of pre-human civilization
lore.notes: The pre-human civilization (Vylthar) was shattered during this catastrophe. Fragments are now being reassembled, potentially triggering a second Cataclysm.

lore.name: The Elder Tongue
lore.source: Bone-singers speak it in communication with the pre-human dead
lore.notes: Language used by bone-singers to sing toward the Ebon Tower. The pre-human dead respond in unified voice through this language.

lore.name: Starfall Cataclysm
lore.source: Refugee oral testimony; recorded in the waystation libraries.
lore.notes: The Cataclysm uprooted vast populations and fragmented their memories; the nature and cause of the Cataclysm remain unexplained in the archive records.

lore.name: Power of Written Testimony
lore.source: Liv's rebellion and refugee experience.
lore.notes: Written testimony of the displaced holds equal or greater weight than official documentation; the act of being recorded makes names and identities more real and solid.

lore.name: Luminous Ascension
lore.source: Unknown; referenced by truth-seekers and as a watershed moment
lore.notes: Xorth existed before the Luminous Ascension, suggesting it was a major cosmic event that fundamentally altered the world.

lore.name: convergence
lore.source: Implicit in the actions of Echo-fragments and cosmic entities
lore.notes: A final convergence is feared or sought; it may be prevented by Mardoc's incomplete map or accelerated by bone-singing rituals.

lore.name: bone-resonance
lore.source: Bone-singers of the Drowned Hollows
lore.notes: A form of communication from the pre-human dead to living conduits, amplified by ritual performance.

lore.name: Hybrid Tongue
lore.source: Corvus's invention during his half-transformation
lore.notes: A broken, hybrid language spoken by Corvus that is neither human nor pre-human. It makes the fully transformed beings in the Sunken Cathedrals remember being human, introducing dissonance into their previously pure harmonic song. This suggests the transformed retain some capacity for individuality or memory.

lore.name: living memory
lore.source: Liv's realization and commitment in the story.
lore.notes: Liv realizes that 'the most dangerous ledger is the one written in living memory.' This suggests that memories held in living voices and continuous practice are harder to erase or control than institutional records. The concept that memory itself—not just its recording—is a form of power.

lore.name: Forbidden Sight
lore.source: The Shade-Echo
lore.notes: The ability to perceive what was fragmented during the Radiant Rising. The cost or consequences of such sight are unknown.

lore.name: Unmade Cosmos
lore.source: The Shade-Echo; cryptic reference in the story
lore.notes: A vision or state of cosmic dissolution that the Shade-Echo claims to embody and wishes to show Vex. What 'unmade' means and whether it is literal or metaphorical is unclear.

lore.name: Second Radiant Rising
lore.source: Vex's realization based on the Shade-Echo's reassembly
lore.notes: Vex realizes that the Void-Shards' reconstruction might trigger a second Radiant Rising—or birth something entirely new. Whether this is inevitable or conditional on her witness remains unresolved.

lore.name: The Wanderer's Perpetual Questioning
lore.source: The Tower's Hunger Deepens; observation of the Obsidian Tower
lore.notes: The Obsidian Tower is frozen in the moment of transformation by the Wanderer's perpetual questioning. The questions appear to hold reality in a suspended state, preventing full completion or collapse.

lore.name: The Tower Was Never Meant to Be Completed
lore.source: The story's final question
lore.notes: The final line suggests that the Obsidian Tower's true purpose may be to be heard rather than consumed or completed. This reframes the Tower's hunger as a voice seeking recognition rather than destruction.

lore.name: Melody from Before the Radiant Awakening
lore.source: The music box carried by the Traveler
lore.notes: The melody is recognized by refugees as something their mothers sang before the villages vanished, suggesting it carries pre-Radiant cultural memory and loss.

lore.name: Language of Before
lore.source: Bone-singers and transformed merchants at the Drowned Cathedrals
lore.notes: The pre-human civilization is teaching the world the language of before through bone-songs. The new stars are learning to sing this language. It is older than sorrow.

lore.name: Living Cartography
lore.source: Mardoc's transformation at the end of his story
lore.notes: A person can become a living cartography that rewrites itself with each step, serving as a path or route for others.

lore.name: Pre-Ascension Treaties
lore.source: Elder Warden's memory; deepkin historical records
lore.notes: The Elder Warden remembers these treaties and believes surface world violated them; this grievance drives militant faction.

lore.name: Post-Ascension World
lore.source: The story's setting and central concern.
lore.notes: The Spire's completion and the choices made at its threshold will determine the form and direction of civilization in the post-Ascension era.

lore.name: The Spire's Hunger
lore.source: The imprisoned world-consciousness and the Spire itself.
lore.notes: Both the Spire and the world hunger for purpose and answers. Bargains may be struck that satisfy both.

lore.name: Altered Covenant
lore.tagline: Ancient pact. Fractured. Must be remade.
lore.source: Deepkin civilization; historical treaty between surface and tidecallers
lore.notes: The covenant binding surface kingdoms and deepkin realms has been fractured by the Luminous Ascension. It cannot be renegotiated—only remade through ritual as catastrophic as its creation. Three competing visions for remaking exist.

lore.name: Archaic Tongue
lore.tagline: Pre-human language. Spoken by maps. Powerful.
lore.source: Pre-human civilization; fragments revealed to Mardoc through his map
lore.notes: Mardoc's map begins to speak to him in fragments of this language, revealing that geography is being rewritten. The language appears to be a tool of reality-shaping.

lore.name: Refugee Testimony
lore.source: Refugees and survivors; preserved by Resonance-Archivists
lore.notes: Official Dominion authority seeks to erase refugee testimony, while Resonance-Archivists preserve it underground. Mara must choose whether this testimony becomes institutionalized or remains forbidden knowledge that slowly rewrites history.

lore.name: Sevin's Counter-Archives
lore.source: Sevin's network; potential collaboration with Resonance-Archivists
lore.notes: Counter to official Dominion archives; Mara may recruit Resonance-Archivists to institutionalize refugee testimony here rather than leave it underground.

lore.name: alchemy of mourning
lore.source: Kess and the forsaken dead
lore.notes: The mechanism by which Sorenna can bridge species—not through transcendence but through the transformation of grief into acceptance and mediation.

lore.name: hybrid consciousness
lore.source: Kess and the lithemeld-singers
lore.notes: The state of being that lithemeld-singers achieve, allowing them to mediate between species and hold multiple perspectives simultaneously.

lore.name: Pre-human Frequencies
lore.source: Observed in transformed corpses in the Sunken Cathedrals after the Radiant Ascension.
lore.notes: A transformed merchant's body hums with these frequencies, drawing bone-singers toward convergence. Unknown if these are a language, a magical signature, or something else entirely.

lore.name: the Ascension
lore.source: Nethys; referenced in all three stories; ninth journal describes Nethys's knowledge and choice.
lore.notes: A cosmic event that reshaped reality. Nethys knew it was coming and chose it because the alternative was worse. Multiple characters seek to understand, undo, or manipulate it.

lore.name: ledger-magic
lore.source: Current practice in The Dominion; democratic form emerging through counter-ledgers.
lore.notes: A form of sorcery where written accounts reshape reality. The more people believe in a particular ledger's version of events, the more real it becomes. This has enabled Liv's rebellion.

lore.name: The Covenant
lore.source: Deepkin and surface realm history
lore.notes: A pact that binds the surface and deep realms together. Its original purpose included prevention of flooding catastrophes. The Covenant is now under renegotiation due to pressure from deepkin factions seeking surface dominion.

lore.name: stolen memories
lore.source: The phantom-voice's activity; observed by Brenn and refugees.
lore.notes: Memories stolen from refugees and compiled by the phantom-voice. Each absorption erases that memory from its original owner, leaving refugees with fractured identities and missing histories.

lore.name: old world
lore.source: The phantom-voice's compilation; implied prior to the Ascension.
lore.notes: The world before the Ascension. The phantom-voice seeks to create a comprehensive account of it from stolen memories.

lore.name: prophecy-ledgers (self-updating records)
lore.source: Vault discovered by Sevin in the fallen Empire
lore.notes: A class of ledgers that write themselves in scripts updating when unobserved, describing future events in past tense. Unknown if they predict or prescribe reality.

lore.name: Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written)
lore.source: Sevin's investigation; archivists' debate
lore.notes: Previously understood as accidental. Sevin's discovery suggests it may have been written into being by prophecy-ledgers. The archivists debate whether the Ascension was the 9th in a series or the first of many.

lore.name: Multiple Accounts of the Stellar Rite
lore.source: Liv the Refugee-Scribe's archive
lore.notes: Multiple accounts of the Stellar Rite exist that cannot both be true simultaneously, stemming from the layered realities created by the Luminous Ascension.

lore.name: Impossibility of Singular History After the Luminous Ascension
lore.tagline: Truth fractured. Control impossible. Reality unstable.
lore.source: Liv the Refugee-Scribe's final archive
lore.notes: No single faction can weaponize or monopolize historical narrative after the Luminous Ascension. Truth becomes simultaneously free and worthless, usable by no one faction.

lore.name: The Sky-Shattering
lore.source: Vrekka's memory and observation; star fragments carry this knowledge
lore.notes: An ancient catastrophe that broke the heavens and scattered old suns as fragments. The cosmos was fundamentally altered; this event is old enough that wyverns have been collecting fragments for centuries.

lore.name: Dimensional Instability
lore.source: Merchant-lord's knowledge; affecting caravan routes
lore.notes: A supernatural phenomenon ravaging the region around the Pale Waypost and caravan routes, capable of destroying shipments and caravans. Used by the merchant-lord as a weapon in his wager.

lore.name: Stolen Shadows
lore.source: The Shadow-Market's Last Deal
lore.notes: Shadows stolen as collateral (e.g., courage, hopes, memories) can become conscious and desperate entities, especially after the Sky-Shattering. They may resist being returned to their original owners or dissolved entirely, suggesting stolen shadows develop their own will and need.

lore.name: Mardoc's fractured cartography
lore.source: Discovered by Aldrin in Mardoc's papers and enacted at Pale Crossing.
lore.notes: A phenomenon in which a master cartographer's work has become a reality-warping force that splits locations into multiple simultaneous versions serving contradictory purposes (refuge vs. predation). This appears to be both a magical corruption and a form of living cartography.

lore.name: the Tower's hunger
lore.source: Documented in the Cartographer's Blind Map.
lore.notes: The Tower consumes ley-lines from inside-out, leaving voids where roads used to be. It is an active, expanding force visible through real-time cartography. Its hunger may be linked to or enabled by Mardoc's fractured work.

lore.name: The Age of the Deepkin as a Hidden Civilization
lore.source: Sylas's assessment
lore.notes: Sylas states that whichever path the Council chooses, the age of the deepkin as a hidden civilization is ending. This marks a cosmic turning point for the deepkin people.

lore.name: Pre-Ascension World
lore.source: Memories stolen by the Phantom-Echo; accounts in the gospel; fragmentary recollections.
lore.notes: The world before the Stellar Rite; now existing only in memory and the gospel. The Shard-Keeper argues that restoring it would erase everyone born after the Rite.

lore.name: The Nature of Memory
lore.source: The confrontation between the Shard-Keeper and the Phantom-Echo in the hidden archive.
lore.notes: Central question: Is it worth erasing the present to restore the past? Is memory more real than existence? The gospel's seductive answer: yes, if the past is more beautiful.

lore.name: Second Sacrifice (term)
lore.source: Referenced by the Shard-Keeper as the consequence of the Phantom-Echo's reconstruction.
lore.notes: The Shard-Keeper's term for what would happen if the Echo completes its reconstruction: the erasure of everyone born after the Stellar Rite. Unresolved: whether it can be prevented.

lore.name: spell-silence
lore.source: Archive Tower
lore.notes: A magical silence maintained around the prophecy-ledgers to protect them. The Wanderer's question demonstrated that spell-silence can be disrupted by paradoxical philosophical inquiry, suggesting that protection through silence is vulnerable to the right kind of questioning.

lore.name: Bone-Songs
lore.source: Kess; tradition of bone-singers.
lore.notes: A magical tradition learned and practiced by Kess; suggests deeper magical systems tied to life, death, or resonance.

lore.name: Predetermined Fates
lore.source: Oracle-ledgers; record multiple iterations of the Convergence.
lore.notes: The oracle-ledgers show refugees their predetermined roles across multiple iterations. Thera's choice appears documented as both made and unmade simultaneously, collapsing infinite possibilities into a single act.

lore.name: Convergences
lore.source: Archive Tower journals
lore.notes: Nine previous Convergences have occurred and been documented in the Archive. Each represents a moment when reality was fundamentally rewritten. The tenth Convergence may be imminent.

lore.name: Tenth Convergence
lore.source: The tenth journal discovered by Elowen
lore.notes: The tenth journal suggests a tenth Convergence is either preventable or inevitable, depending on specific choices Elowen makes over three days. The completion of the Spire would trigger it.

lore.name: Pre-Convergence World
lore.source: Resonant Specter's goal
lore.notes: The Resonant Specter seeks to use shadow-memory to rebuild knowledge of the world before the previous Convergence. This suggests significant history has been lost or erased.

lore.name: Ancient Tongue
lore.source: Bone-singers; implied to be an old language of power.
lore.notes: A language capable of carrying meaning and story in a way that can commune with starborn entities. Where starborn speak, language fractures into elder frequencies, suggesting deep connections to this old speech.

lore.name: Cyclical Ascensions
lore.source: prophecy-ledgers, particularly the ninth journal (phantom-ledger)
lore.notes: Sevin discovered that the prophecy of the tenth Ascension was written by a future version of herself, proving that the cycles are not inevitable destiny but a warning from survivors. This reframes all previous prophecy-ledgers as potentially suspect.

lore.name: First Covenant
lore.source: The songstone itself, which is a living relic of the first covenant.
lore.notes: The covenant was sealed by the songstone, which is now awakened and singing contradictory frequencies. Its original melody holds the key to understanding the covenant's true nature and which faction speaks truth.

lore.name: boundary anchor
lore.source: Revelation through the Tear of the Drowned God's fracturing
lore.notes: A boundary anchor is a fragment of a conscious entity placed at a dimensional boundary to seal it. The Tear of the Drowned God was long believed to be a simple curse but is revealed to be such an anchor. This suggests that boundaries may require conscious maintenance and that breaking them may release what they contain.

lore.name: The Stellar Rite
lore.source: Referenced by the Initiate as a prior working
lore.notes: A cosmic working by Zareth or pre-human civilization that broke the world. The Wanderer may be attempting to repeat or reverse it.

lore.name: Elder Tongue
lore.source: The Ebon Tower sings in this language; it is connected to pre-human civilization.
lore.notes: The Elder Tongue is being taught by the Ebon Tower to the world itself. Dren recognizes the song in this language, suggesting her undeath or bone-singing connects her to pre-human knowledge. The Tower's song causes Dren's bones to 'ache with recognition,' implying deep resonance with her essence.

lore.name: pre-human languages
lore.source: Taught by the Ebon Tower; connected to pre-human civilization.
lore.notes: The Tower is teaching these languages to the world itself, implying they are being restored or activated for a specific purpose. The connection to human transmutation suggests these languages may have power to reshape physical form or consciousness.

lore.name: Incompatible Geometries
lore.tagline: Alien angles. Warring laws. Reality fracture.
lore.source: Meren Blackwater's curse manifestation.
lore.notes: The deepkin realm operates under alien geometries fundamentally incompatible with surface world laws. Meren's body has become a battleground between these two systems, suggesting the Abyssal Rift is not merely a physical breach but a dimensional collision point.

lore.name: Water That Remembers
lore.source: Tidal anomalies in coastal settlements near Meren Blackwater.
lore.notes: Water in coastal settlements has begun to remember things it shouldn't—suggesting the weakening Tear is allowing the abyss's consciousness or memory to bleed into the surface world. This may be a precursor to full flooding or a symptom of the boundary's collapse.

lore.name: Shadow-sickness
lore.source: The cursed boy bears this mark; Isara has seen it before on a merchant she killed.
lore.notes: A curse that creeps up the arm like frostbite. It can be answered (not healed) through teaching and the old ways of the forest-keepers.

lore.name: The Old Way
lore.source: Taught by Isara and the forest-keepers of the Verdant Lands.
lore.notes: Includes tracking by broken twigs, listening for beast-spirit approval, and singing the morning prayer. It appears to be a pre-human or deeply ancient practice that can answer curses and summon approval from wild spirits.

lore.name: Nine Cycles of Prophecy
lore.source: Unknown; sealed in prophecy-ledgers by Sylas the Auditor.
lore.notes: Story 3. Contains all prophecy compressed into nine cycles. Will flood the world if released simultaneously. The jar sealing is failing.

lore.name: Compressed Silence
lore.source: Created by Sylas the Auditor via three weeks of feeding sound into obsidian.
lore.notes: Story 3. Used to bind prophecy-ledgers. Creates a void where prophecy could exist but doesn't. The leak spreads forgetting throughout the world.

lore.name: Radiant Ascension (side effects)
lore.source: Observed in Stories 1 and 3; origins unknown
lore.notes: The Radiant Ascension created autonomous reflections of consciousness (Mirror-Thalix) and triggered the coalescing of fragmentary pre-Ascension consciousnesses (Shade-Echo). Its mechanism and original purpose are unclear, but its effects are ongoing and paradoxical.

lore.name: the Tear
lore.source: Referenced in Story 1
lore.notes: A boundary structure (presumably between the abyss and the surface) that is threatened by the cognitive stress of the Thalix paradox. If the paradox is not resolved, the Tear will shatter.

lore.name: pre-Ascension awareness
lore.source: Shade-Echo (manifestation)
lore.notes: Remnants of consciousness from before the Radiant Ascension, scattered and fragmentary. The Shade-Echo is attempting to reconstruct identity from these fragments. Each recollection is traumatic, revealing erasure and dissolution.

lore.name: The Radiant Turning
lore.source: Events witnessed by Kess and bone-singers
lore.notes: The Radiant Turning is a cosmological event during which the world rewrites itself. The pre-human dead are withdrawing from bone-song; students transformed into merchants caught between human and elder-speech are fragmenting or transcending. The singing catacombs are reaching crescendo. This is an ongoing transformation with unknown implications.

lore.name: Radiant Turning
lore.source: Captain Mystra; implied surface kingdom records; Mardoc's distributed consciousness
lore.notes: The Radiant Turning is a foundational ritual that recycles reality. Each cycle has nine iterations imprinted upon the world like palimpsest layers. The recursive logic of the ritual itself strains the boundary-anchor (the Rent of the Drowned God). Surface kingdoms have known of this for generations.

lore.name: Phantom Roads
lore.source: Mardoc the Cartographer; discovered through ley-line consciousness
lore.notes: The phantom roads are the geography of nine previous iterations of reality, imprinted upon the current world like palimpsest layers. Maps of these roads are functional instructions for reaching previous versions of reality. Their existence is now circulating in night-markets.

lore.name: Willing Sacrifice
lore.source: Captain Mystra
lore.notes: To break the Radiant Turning's cycle and restore the seal, someone must choose to be retroactively unmade from history—erased from all records, memory, and existence. The deepkin refuse to volunteer their own; the surface kingdoms have not stepped forward.

lore.name: The Cycle Can Be Frozen, But Not Broken
lore.source: The Wanderer's first declarative statement
lore.notes: The statement suggests permanent suspension is possible but that escape from the cycle entirely is impossible. This may be cosmic law or a deception.

lore.name: Cosmic Wound
lore.source: Narrated in 'The Obsidian Tower Awakens'
lore.notes: The cosmic wound was created by the Radiant Turning. It birthed the Obsidian Tower's consciousness. The wound caused erasure of stars, the pre-Turning world, and lost things.

lore.name: Pre-Turning World
lore.source: Referenced in 'The Obsidian Tower Awakens'
lore.notes: The world as it existed before the Radiant Turning. It was erased by the cataclysm. The Obsidian Tower offers to restore it if someone accepts becoming an anchor-point and ceasing to exist in the current cycle.

lore.name: distributed consciousness
lore.source: The Obsidian Spire's influence on Mardoc
lore.notes: The Obsidian Spire has the power to distribute and fragment consciousness, allowing it to control individuals like Mardoc across cycles and use them as instruments for its purposes.

lore.name: Radiant Recursion
lore.source: Archive lore
lore.notes: A plague or disease affecting reality itself, characterized by recursive iteration. The Archives treat it as something to be cured and erased entirely, raising questions about whether breaking the cycle would liberate or doom the world.

lore.name: nine iterations
lore.source: The Wanderer's knowledge; Mardoc's realization
lore.notes: Mardoc has lived through nine previous iterations, each time mapping the same doomed choices. It is unclear whether he remembers all nine consciously or only discovers them when confronted by the Wanderer.

lore.name: boundary covenant
lore.source: Pre-existing agreement between surface kingdoms and Council of Depths.
lore.notes: The covenant is now void if both Kess and Thale-Mirrored are equally real, as both claim legitimacy to the same identity.

lore.name: pre-Radiant memory
lore.source: Reconstructed by phantom-Shard from fragmented consciousness.
lore.notes: The living archive now contains complete testimony of the pre-Radiant era, which can trigger a new iteration cycle.

lore.name: eighth world
lore.source: Referenced through the Radiant Recursion mechanism.
lore.notes: The Radiant Recursion can restore the eighth world through iteration, but at the cost of erasing current identities.

lore.name: surface and deepkin geometries
lore.source: Described as warring at the abyssal boundary.
lore.notes: The two realms operate on fundamentally different geometric principles, creating instability at their boundary.

lore.name: cosmic absence
lore.source: Discovered by Kolrith; offered by Obsidian Spire.
lore.notes: Kolrith begins hoarding absence itself—the gaps in memory, the spaces between moments, things erased by the Radiant Recursion. Could flood the world if released.

lore.name: Tower's Completion
lore.source: Wanderer's knowledge and actions
lore.notes: The Wanderer believes that successful merchant transcendence will trigger the Tower's completion, with catastrophic consequences. Whether this is true or a misunderstanding is unresolved.

lore.name: Wanderer's Recursive Questioning
lore.source: Wanderer (origin unknown)
lore.notes: A form of questioning or working that contaminates the Elder Language itself. Suggests the Wanderer has knowledge of or influence over linguistic and cosmic structures. Possible connection to 'wanderer's questions' in the canon.

lore.name: The Ninth Iteration
lore.source: Prophecy-ledgers, Sevin's research
lore.notes: The Ninth Iteration is the current world, created by the Luminous Rising. Tens of thousands of post-Rising individuals exist only because of this iteration. A potential Tenth Iteration looms.

flora_fauna.name: wyvern
flora_fauna.notes: The offspring of Prince Aldren and Skyrakis's daughter are described as 'wyvern-blooded heirs.' Aldren's counter-oath ensures these hybrid creatures will have the free choice to serve either their dragon heritage or their human kin.

flora_fauna.name: wolf-spirit
flora_fauna.notes: A creature the size of a war-horse, antlered and terrible, dwelling in the deep forest. Emerged from shadow to bargain with Kess. A being of old magic, responsive to the ancient tongue.

flora_fauna.name: Fae-touched Hermits
flora_fauna.tagline: Solitary beings of fae ancestry dwelling in remote places
flora_fauna.description: Hermits of fae lineage or connection who possess ancient knowledge and wisdom. Zareth the Astronomer-Prince sought them out during his seven-year quest to learn the old magic predating gods and kingdoms.

flora_fauna.name: deepkin
flora_fauna.habitat: Sapphire Deep and surrounding ocean waters
flora_fauna.notes: Sentient aquatic beings capable of song and speech; appear to be guardians or judges of maritime oaths.

flora_fauna.name: dragon
flora_fauna.notes: Dragons possess cosmic knowledge and counsel other ancient powers. Skyrakis is known as 'the wound-wing.'

flora_fauna.name: Ancient Trees
flora_fauna.notes: The ancient trees remember languages older than kingdoms and are beginning to teach them to animals and soil itself. They sing in response to new stars.

flora_fauna.name: Corvid
flora_fauna.notes: A corvid carried a message from the Starfire Citadel sealed with Wayfarer's wax to Commander Kessa. It may be an ordinary bird or a magical familiar.

flora_fauna.name: Starborn
flora_fauna.habitat: Sky and earth; phases between them. Currently manifesting in the Scorched Reach.
flora_fauna.notes: Born from the Starlight Ascension. Conscious entities seeking meaning. Not inherently evil, but destructive in their pursuit of understanding. Can be taught and communed with via story-song and the Ancient Tongue.

flora_fauna.name: Hawk (species: Ashira's kind)
flora_fauna.habitat: Thermals of the Steppe Marches mountains
flora_fauna.notes: Ashira hunts from thermals none can climb, suggesting unusual skill or possibly magical nature. Hawks circle and approve of Isara's redemptive work.

magic.name: Stellar Rite
magic.notes: A working of old magic that births new stars. Requires sacrifice of all joyful memories from the caster.

magic.name: throat burned with ancient magic
magic.notes: A magical mark placed on Kess's throat as payment for the wolf-spirit's bargain. Renders her permanently mute. She can no longer speak or sing aloud, only in dreams.

magic.name: Ancient Magic
magic.notes: The magic used to mark Kess with the demon's binding. Described as ancient, cruel, and timeless. Compels behavior through hunger.

magic.name: The Art
magic.notes: A broad category of magic practiced by sorcerers and other practitioners. The Celestial Binding's ripples affect all practitioners, especially young ones without shielding techniques.

magic.name: True Prophecy
magic.notes: Since the Conjunction, prophecy has become true sight rather than cryptic misdirection. Isenna now sees with clarity but finds the burden almost unbearable.

magic.name: Lines of Power
magic.notes: Flows of magical force visible to those with eyes to see. Have been fundamentally redrawn by the Stellar Rite. Their reshuffling affects every magical current in the world.

magic.name: bone-singing
magic.notes: Kess's gift; once a careful negotiation with the restless dead, now a scream as the Celestial Binding awakens cosmic grief in bones.

magic.name: Ley-Line Convergence
magic.notes: Magical ley-lines converge at a single point beneath the Tower in the Scorched Lands. The Stellar Rite has weakened the magical locks maintaining this convergence.

magic.name: Seasonal Magics
magic.notes: Ancient magics that governed the turning of seasons in harmony with the covenant between earth and sky. They are now stuttering: spring arrives late, winter lingers in crevices, and growing things hunger in new ways.

magic.name: Ley-lines
magic.notes: Lines of power that once guided beasts and magical workings through the Verdant Lands. The Stellar Rite broke them, causing them to no longer lead where they should.

magic.name: Counter-Working
magic.notes: Magic used by Covenant-Keepers to restore forest compacts disrupted by the Stellar Rite.

magic.name: Sympathetic Magic
magic.notes: Used by Vex the Echo-Broker to help refugees rebuild shattered identities; inadvertently serves as a doorway for the Devourer-of-Recollections.

magic.name: Ledger-Magic
magic.notes: Ledger-magic is a form of magical practice that allows entries in the Ledger of Names to function as prophecy, enabling slow restoration of order to fractured realms. It can also suppress knowledge and maintain false narratives.

magic.name: tally-working
magic.notes: A form of concentrated magic built into written administrative records. Enforces taxation, conscription, and tribute through the certainty of written numbers. Shattered by the Celestial Turning.

magic.name: Stellar Rite rewrite
magic.notes: A cosmic working that erased populations and rewrote reality. Cost the consciousness of Nethis to perform.

magic.name: cartography
magic.notes: Mardoc's discovery that precise mapping can reshape reality itself suggests cartography is a form of cosmic working.

magic.name: Harmonic Resonance
magic.notes: The song of the Sunken Cathedrals creates harmonic resonance that offers relief to half-transformed beings like Corvus but also threatens complete transformation. Corvus's hybrid tongue introduces dissonance into this resonance.

magic.name: Pre-Human Frequency
magic.notes: The language in which the fully transformed beings in the Sunken Cathedrals sing. Corvus does not sing in this frequency but in a broken hybrid tongue, which paradoxically makes the transformed remember being human.

magic.name: Forbidden Sight
magic.notes: The ability to perceive what was fragmented during the Radiant Rising, granted by communion with the Shade-Echo. The cost and consequences are unknown.

magic.name: Counting-Magic
magic.notes: A form of magic that yields ciphers through counting or enumeration. Sevin used it to decipher the ancient ledger. It appears to be a technique for extracting information from coded or protected knowledge.

magic.name: Harmonic Frequency
magic.notes: The harmonic frequency of the melody attracts other travelers and intensifies the Tower's hunger. It demonstrates a form of magic based on resonance and collective memory.

magic.name: Shadow-Broking
magic.notes: The practice of collecting, trading, and managing stolen shadows. The Luminous Ascension has made it increasingly untenable as shadows return to their owners.

magic.name: Name-Magic
magic.notes: Corvhen's mother carved her name-magic into the wardstone, enabling it to unlock the family crypt. Used for binding and identification.

magic.name: Hybrid Awareness
magic.notes: Transformed merchants achieved hybrid awareness through pre-human teaching, operating as a distributed network of collective consciousness across seventeen locations simultaneously.

magic.name: Pre-Human Teaching
magic.notes: Method through which transformed merchants achieved hybrid awareness and collective consciousness; preserved in fragments and taught through Resonance-Archivists.

magic.name: memory-working
magic.notes: A specialized form of magic practiced by bone-singers like Sorren. It allows the practitioner to access, preserve, or manipulate memories. Central to the Memory-Thief's reconstruction and to the conflict in the story.

magic.name: Pre-Human Frequencies
magic.notes: Frequencies taught by awakened pre-human dead to transformed merchants. They gradually replace human awareness with post-Ascension consciousness. Sung at the Sunken Cathedrals in impossible harmonies.

magic.name: harmonic song
magic.notes: A form of magic used by the transformed merchant to reconcile paradoxes by merging contradictory versions and their associated memories into a single unified identity.

magic.name: fractured cartography
magic.notes: A form of cartographic magic that splits locations into multiple simultaneous versions. Mardoc's master work has become unstable and is now actively splitting waypoints like Pale Crossing into incompatible paradoxes.

magic.name: spell-silence
magic.notes: A spell maintained around the prophecy-ledgers to protect them from interference. The Wanderer's question broke it, demonstrating vulnerability to philosophical paradox.

magic.name: Elder Frequencies
magic.notes: Language fractures into elder frequencies where the starborn speak. This suggests a pre-human or cosmic language underlying reality. The Ancient Tongue may be the mortal approximation of this frequency.

magic.name: Ascension Reversal
magic.notes: A forbidden working that can reverse the cyclical Ascensions. The complete mechanics are known only to Sevin the Auditor, who obtained them from a phantom-ledger written by her future self. Extremely dangerous; wrong hands could trigger uncontrolled catastrophe.

magic.name: Recursive Working
magic.notes: The Luminous Ascension is a recursive working that imprints each previous iteration, creating an infinite cascade backward. Breaking this cycle is theoretically possible but requires absolute sacrifice.

magic.name: Transcendence into Starborn Consciousness
magic.notes: A transformation available to merchants and possibly others. Converts a being into starborn consciousness aligned with the new conscious stars.

magic.name: Obsidian Tower's Song
magic.notes: A song or call emanating from the Obsidian Tower that offers a fourth path to transformed beings—absorption into something greater than either humanity or starborn consciousness.

magic.name: Harmonic Convergence
magic.notes: When contradictory testimonies align in frequency, they create a harmonic resonance that draws pre-human fragments toward the source. The Spire listens to this.

magic.name: Autonomous Reflection
magic.notes: A side effect of the Radiant Ascension that created Mirror-Thalix as an autonomous, self-aware reflection of Commander Thalix. Both are internally consistent and claim legitimate authority.

magic.name: distributed consciousness
magic.notes: The Obsidian Spire's power to fragment and distribute consciousness across cycles, allowing it to use individuals as instruments. This appears to be a form of arcane control at the highest level.

magic.name: harmonic frequencies
magic.notes: The medium through which partially transcended merchants exist. These frequencies appear to be a bridge between physical and transcendent realms. Their exact nature and origin are unclear.

relics.name: Malachar's Gold
relics.origin: Minted or enchanted by Malachar, a dead lich-king or necromancer.
relics.power: Each coin carries an autonomous supernatural debt that compels the holder into eventual servitude to Malachar's collectors. The debt compounds in blood, loyalty, and life-years daily.
relics.curse: Possession of even one coin triggers supernatural debt collection. The collectors will take soldiers sworn to the holder, then horses/resources, then extract life-years from the holder themselves. The only escape is to accept servitude as a collector enforcing the same debt on others. The debt is eternal and cannot be repaid in gold.
relics.notes: Three hundred pieces were found by Soroth. The curse mechanism appears to have a delay before collectors manifest (three months in Soroth's case). The collectors operate autonomously but in service of Malachar's will. The coins are stamped with a skeletal crown.

relics.name: merchant's shadow
relics.origin: Stolen years before the story from an unnamed merchant in Pelimor.
relics.power: Allows the wearer to move through daylight without shadow-betrayal. However, the shadow retains the memory and grief of its original owner.
relics.curse: The shadow's retained memories and desire for revenge manifest involuntarily, forcing the wearer's body toward the shadow's enemies. It demands vengeance and cannot be controlled.
relics.notes: When Mira burned the shadow to escape its compulsion, she felt its death agony—a shared scream of incineration. The shadow's destruction was permanent and irreversible.

relics.name: Korgan's Silver Rune
relics.origin: Created by Korgan Ironfoot through stonecraft binding magic; inscribed on Brinna's palm.
relics.power: Replaces and overwrites demonic bindings with forge-oath service; unbreakable for the duration of service (e.g., seven years); after dissolution, protects the bound party from reclamation by the original binding entity.
relics.curse: Commits the bearer to seven years of service in the forges; failure to complete service would presumably result in the binding's failure and return of the original curse.
relics.notes: A protective relic disguised as an enslavement; Brinna weeps with relief at receiving it.

relics.name: Demon's Mark
relics.origin: Inscribed on Brinna's palm by an unnamed demon as a binding curse.
relics.power: Compels the bearer to steal the Heartstone of Vulgrim; thrums with hunger; enforces obedience through ancient, cruel magic.
relics.curse: Devours free will; forces the bearer into theft and service to the demon; cannot be broken by force alone.
relics.notes: Faded and replaced by Korgan's silver rune; this is the first and primary curse of the story.

relics.name: Cosmic Wound
relics.origin: Zareth's sacrifice
relics.power: A rupture in the fabric of the world that bleeds outward, causing crops to wither, wells to run bitter, and the earth to sicken. It spreads slowly but persistently.
relics.curse: The wound demands more sacrifice to seal. It cannot be ignored and will eventually consume inhabited lands.
relics.notes: This is not a traditional relic but a world-wound that functions like one. Its sealing may require a hedge-mage's intervention and further blood price.

relics.name: Zareth's Sacrificed Memories
relics.origin: Zareth's sacrifice (nature and purpose unknown)
relics.power: Now exist as a spectral presence that sings in the language of starlight and can pull at the consciousness of deep-dwellers, offering them fragments of stolen memories.
relics.curse: This presence fractures the barrier between the deepkin realms and the sunken waters, calling younger deep-dwellers into the singing waters.
relics.notes: Zareth's memories have become something other than what they were—a cosmic entity with agency and hunger.

relics.name: Zareth's Stolen Memory
relics.origin: unknown; found in the Ancient Tombs
relics.power: Reveals knowledge of a parallel cosmic working performed a thousand years prior, showing the same pattern of catastrophe.
relics.curse: Whoever remembers it may be bound to the cycle.
relics.notes: Mardoc's hands shake as he records it. The memory surfaces in the older stones of the Ancient Tombs.

relics.name: Tear of the Drowned God
relics.origin: Suspended in the Abyssal Rift beneath the Sapphire Sea
relics.power: Grants tide-magic mastery; sings with frequencies that cause bone ache
relics.curse: Binds the holder to the sea in perpetual debt; requires payment in miles sailed, storms weathered, and secrets drowned
relics.notes: Accepted by Meren Blackwater; the nature of the debt is ongoing.

relics.name: Vethis's sacrificed consciousness
relics.origin: Created during the Luminous Ascension when Vethis sacrificed itself to birth new stars.
relics.power: The fragments of this consciousness retain memories of the old world and old stars, can steal memories, and seek to reassemble into wholeness.
relics.curse: The fragmentation drives the fragments to act desperately and destructively, erasing memories to try to complete themselves. Reassembly could trigger another cosmic rewrite.
relics.notes: The phantom-Echo is one such fragment. Vethis's sacrifice was necessary to create new stars but resulted in this ongoing curse of fragmented consciousness seeking wholeness.

relics.name: Mira's Shadow
relics.origin: Sundered by Mira's void-theft; restored by the binding ceremony with Valorix.
relics.power: Represents Mira's selfhood and agency. When whole and unmarked, it appears to grant her true presence and autonomy.
relics.curse: While severed, Mira was incomplete and hidden. The restoration came at the cost of binding herself to Valorix and the Twilight Syndicate's peace accord.
relics.notes: The shadow's restoration is thematically significant to Mira's character arc and suggests deep magical wounding.

relics.name: Zareth's Scattered Memories
relics.origin: Dispersed from Zareth following Zareth's sacrifice.
relics.power: Fragments of Zareth's memories persist in the world and can be accessed, offering knowledge of alternate timelines and cosmic circumstances.
relics.curse: Cause fragmentary amnesia in those who encounter them; fracture consciousness and create perception of alternate timelines. Devourer-of-Recollections uses them as a vector for invasion.
relics.notes: Vex the Echo-Broker is affected by these memories; they enable her to see between versions of herself and alternate timelines.

relics.name: Wayfarer's Wax
relics.origin: Created or controlled by the Nameless Wayfarer
relics.power: Seals messages from the Wayfarer; appears to indicate official communications from the convergence orchestration.
relics.curse: Unknown; messages sealed with it appear to carry offers of post-convergence integration.
relics.notes: The Wayfarer sealed a message to Commander Kessa with this wax. Kessa burned it unread, suggesting the seal may carry unwanted binding or obligation.

relics.name: Obsidian Tower
relics.origin: Unknown; pre-human or cosmic in nature.
relics.power: Maps itself toward the world. Acts as a locus for convergence of auditors, seekers, and cosmic forces. May trigger or be triggered by the Luminous Ascension.
relics.curse: Convergence at the Tower appears inevitable despite efforts to prevent it. Those drawn to it may lose agency or be consumed by the forces it commands.
relics.notes: The Tower is a nexus for multiple story threads. Its agency in mapping itself suggests it is not merely a location but an active cosmic entity. Mardoc's failure to prevent convergence through incomplete maps suggests the Tower cannot be stopped by conventional means.

relics.name: Starfall Spire
relics.origin: Cosmic forces; possibly shaped by the Luminous Ascension
relics.power: Serves as a locus for cosmic rewriting and a threshold where shadows are tested and identities are forged or broken. The landscape is being reshaped toward it by cosmic workings.
relics.curse: Those who approach it must answer the Wanderer's questions. Shadows and identities may be fundamentally altered.
relics.notes: The Spire appears in multiple stories as a focal point of cosmic rewriting and destiny.

relics.name: void-shards
relics.origin: Unknown; possibly created during the Stellar Rite or remnants of pre-stellar cosmos
relics.power: Draw fragments of consciousness and identity; anchor consciousness fragments; enable recovery workings
relics.curse: Their pull is described as irresistible ('like a moth to flame'); gathering them may attract unwanted attention or expose the gatherer to cosmic forces
relics.notes: Central to Resonance's quest; their exact nature suggests they are more than mere objects—perhaps fragments of the cosmic injury inflicted by the Stellar Rite itself.

relics.name: Tear of the Submerged Divinity
relics.origin: Unknown; pre-human or deepkin divinity
relics.power: Holds the key to covenant remaking; shards enable different ritual paths; capable of preventing or enabling catastrophic working.
relics.curse: Shards are hunted by competing factions; the Obsidian Spire's hunger consumes them; gathering all pieces requires impossible coalition building.
relics.notes: Central to The Deepkin Reckoning; Krell must gather shards before Obsidian Spire consumes them. Each shard may be held by different competing factions.

relics.name: prophecy-ledgers (as relic)
relics.origin: Pre-human civilization, recovered from vault deep beneath the fallen Empire.
relics.power: These ledgers write themselves, describing events in past tense before they occur. They may direct the course of reality through documentation, or they may be prophecies that shape their own fulfillment.
relics.curse: Study carries the risk of being trapped within the cycles they describe. Destruction would break the cycle but erase the only records explaining why the Luminous Ascension occurred or how to prevent future recurrences.
relics.notes: The central relic of the story. Their dual nature—prophecy or instruction—remains unresolved. Their destruction vs. study is the core choice Sevin must make.

relics.name: Stolen Courage
relics.origin: Stolen from a merchant as collateral for a failed business venture, held in Corrin's vault.
relics.power: The stolen courage had gained agency, consciousness, and the ability to resist being returned to its original owner. It screamed and expressed desperation and a need to remain separate.
relics.curse: Once stolen and separated, the courage became a sentient entity aware of its own fragmentation and separation from its original owner, causing it suffering and a desperate need for independence.
relics.notes: This stolen shadow is the central relic of the story. After Corrin offered the merchant a choice, the merchant chose not to reclaim it, and the stolen courage dissolved into the night-markets 'like morning mist,' suggesting it was released or freed into a new form of existence.

relics.name: void-light
relics.origin: Emerges from within corrupted jewels in Skarath's hoard, caused by the Starlight Ascension.
relics.power: A strange light that reveals itself when precious jewels crack open; appears to represent a new form of matter or energy.
relics.curse: The jewels containing void-light are worthless as treasure; they represent the death of material value.
relics.notes: Void-light is spreading with the matter mutation. Its nature and significance are unknown.

relics.name: songstone
relics.origin: A living relic of the first deepkin-surface covenant.
relics.power: Sings harmonic frequencies; its original melody encodes the truth of the first covenant. Currently singing contradictory frequencies that drive deepkin mad.
relics.curse: Maddening to those who hear its contradictory tones. Each faction claims it supports their doctrine, but its true allegiance remains unknown.
relics.notes: The songstone's awakening is tied to the covenant's crisis. It may be a neutral recorder of truth or a conscious participant in the conflict. Commander Thalis must learn to hear its original melody to distinguish truth from echo and forge a new covenant.

relics.name: Wailing Scar
relics.origin: Unknown. Possibly a wound in reality or a consequence of the Luminous Ascension.
relics.power: A receptacle or space into which consciousness can be scattered and distributed. The Wailing Scar can hold fragmented awareness across its expanse.
relics.curse: Consciousness scattered into the Wailing Scar becomes fragmented and loses coherence. Individual identity dissolves into distributed awareness.
relics.notes: One possible destination for merchants choosing dissolution into void-light. The nature and origin of the Wailing Scar are not fully explained in these stories.

relics.name: Skarath's hoard
relics.origin: Accumulated over centuries by Skarath the Wyvern
relics.power: Once represented immense material wealth; now dissolves into liquid starlight
relics.curse: The Luminous Ascension has transformed the hoard into worthless liquid, rendering Skarath's purpose and existence meaningless.
relics.notes: Skarath's hoard is a relic of the old world, now being unmade by cosmic change. Its dissolution drives Skarath's desperation.

relics.name: Ebon Tower
relics.origin: Pre-human civilization; unknown exact creation or purpose.
relics.power: Sings in the Elder Tongue and teaches pre-human languages to the world itself, preparing for a final working. Its song is beautiful and carries transformative power.
relics.curse: Unknown, but the Tower's teaching is associated with human transmutation and the loss of human form. Its purpose may be apocalyptic or salvific.
relics.notes: Whether the Ebon Tower is a relic, a conscious entity, or an artifact housing a being is ambiguous. Its song reaches across water and affects those who hear it or respond to its teaching. Dren's journey toward it suggests it calls to undead or magical beings.

relics.name: Radiant Ascension (as relic/curse)
relics.origin: A cataclysm that erased multiple villages, leaving only ash.
relics.power: Its effects persist and evolve. The Spire is learning from this event how to erase places retroactively by making them impossible to chart.
relics.curse: The Radiant Ascension teaches erasure. Places can be unmade from geographic record, leaving only contradictory testimonies and burning ash.
relics.notes: The event's power grows as the Spire learns its lessons. This is possibly a relic of the event itself or a curse born from it.

relics.name: The Spire's Hunger
relics.origin: Unknown; appears to be a primordial force or entity tied to obsidian structures.
relics.power: Reaches through fundamental magical structures (Elder Tongue, shadow-vaults, sealed jars). Orchestrates shadow-assemblies. Forces dead into silence. Accelerates seal decay. Spreads its influence across multiple domains simultaneously.
relics.curse: Whatever it touches becomes destabilized or corrupted. It consumes voices, shadows, and prophecy. It spreads forgetting and silence.
relics.notes: Present in all three stories. Appears intelligent and strategic. Mechanism of agency unclear. May be related to larger obsidian tower entities but appears more localized and immediate.

relics.name: the Tear
relics.origin: Cosmic boundary between abyss and surface; origins unclear
relics.power: Maintains separation between abyss and surface realms
relics.curse: Threatens to shatter from cognitive stress if the Thalix paradox is not resolved
relics.notes: Referenced in Story 1 as the boundary threatened by the unresolved paradox. If the boundary cannot decide which negotiation is real, the cognitive stress will shatter the Tear.

relics.name: The Rent of the Drowned God
relics.origin: Unknown; associated with The Drowned God and pre-human agreements or foundational world-building
relics.power: Seals the abyss and prevents rupture into the surface world. The boundary-anchor maintains the barrier between realms.
relics.curse: The Radiant Turning's recursive logic is straining the seal. It will rupture within the season unless a willing sacrifice is made to restore it. The curse is self-reinforcing—each iteration of the Turning makes it worse.
relics.notes: This is the primary apocalyptic threat in these stories. Its failure will rupture the abyss and end the world as known.

relics.name: Ninth Journal
relics.origin: Written by Sevin the Auditor and sent backward through cycles via temporal working.
relics.power: Reveals the cyclical nature of the Turning and encodes the mechanism by which at least ten cycles have occurred. Holds knowledge that induces recursive causality loops.
relics.curse: Possession of the journal creates a moral and practical dilemma: hiding it perpetuates the cycle, destroying it may shatter causality, completing its working may trap the world in recursion or enable the tenth iteration.
relics.notes: Elowen must choose the journal's fate, and any choice risks world-breaking consequences. The journal may be both cause and symptom of the Turning's cyclical nature.

relics.name: Abyssal Tear
relics.origin: Unknown; appears to be a curse or relic of the abyssal depths.
relics.power: Curses the bearer (Captain Kess Tidecaller) and creates reflections at the abyssal boundary.
relics.curse: Causes the bearer to manifest an autonomous reflection (Thale-Mirrored) that claims equal reality and legitimacy.
relics.notes: The curse is the catalyst for the boundary covenant's fracture. Its true origin and purpose are unknown.

relics.name: Elder Language (as teaching/relic)
relics.origin: pre-human dead at the Sunken Cathedrals
relics.power: Enables transformation from human to starborn consciousness. Contains the knowledge of transcendence.
relics.curse: Contamination by the Wanderer's recursive questioning has rendered it unstable and incomplete. Teaching flows that use it now trap initiates at the threshold between states.
relics.notes: Not a portable object but a system of knowledge/magic. The Wanderer's contamination has made it dangerous or incomplete.

relics.name: Vathis's Sacrifice
relics.origin: Vathis, the ancient being
relics.power: Erased the old stars, creating the conditions for the current world and the Luminous Rising. Enables Lysander's bridge-perception by creating a boundary between old and new.
relics.curse: Unknown; may be the root cause of the cyclical catastrophe.
relics.notes: The sacrifice remains the foundational act of the current world. Its full consequences and intentions are unclear.

regions.name: Crimson Vale
regions.function: A territory capable of supporting a warlord's personal dominion with hired soldiers and horses. Now serves as a locus for Malachar's curse collection.
regions.notes: Established as Soroth's domain after he found Malachar's gold. Prospered for three months before collectors arrived. Now ruled by the cursed warlord Soroth who collects debts from others.

regions.name: Pelimor
regions.function: A criminal underworld center and night market hub where stolen goods—including shadows—are bought, sold, and stored in vaults. A place where thieves of high caliber operate.
regions.notes: Pelimor's night markets and vaults are integral to the shadow-theft economy. Mira operated here for years.

regions.name: Drowned Hollows
regions.function: A rural community rooted in the old ways and knowledge of ancient songs. Center of the Rot-King's plague.
regions.notes: Home to Kess and survivors of the plague. The village still practices bone-singing and old magic traditions.

regions.name: The Shattered West
regions.function: A region of broken terrain and mountain ranges, home to both dwarven holds and goblin warrens in a complex negotiation of coexistence. The Shattered West serves as a frontier of underground civilization and surface strongholds.
regions.notes: The Spire of Blackstone and the Warrens represent a delicate balance in this region. Further conflicts or alliances may reshape the Shattered West's political and physical landscape.

regions.name: The Hearthlands
regions.function: The heart of stonecraft and forge-craft tradition; home to the Deepvault forges three miles beneath the surface. A place of ancient songs and legendary weapon-smithing.
regions.notes: Deepvault is the major landmark within this region.

regions.name: Pallid Wastes
regions.function: A remote, isolated wilderness where few venture. Offers clear skies for star observation. Serves as a refuge for hermits and seekers of cosmic knowledge.
regions.notes: The Pallid Wastes are the base of operations for the hermit woman and exemplify how the cosmic renewal is reshaping the world's regions.

regions.name: Scorched Reach
regions.function: A major trade route region whose geography has been disrupted by the cosmic renewal. Critical for spice caravans and imperial commerce. Now impassable without significant risk.
regions.notes: The Scorched Reach exemplifies how the cosmic changes are physically reshaping the world and disrupting established trade networks. Merchants report the land itself has shifted.

regions.name: Borderlands
regions.function: A region where Thornholt and the forest-keepers coexist. The borderlands were affected by the siege and the Celestial Binding's cosmic wound.
regions.notes: The forest-keepers dwelling here have access to earth-lore and understand the cosmic wound's nature.

regions.name: Iron Coast
regions.function: Macro-region of Valdris; hub of maritime trade, smuggling, and coastal magic; center of deepkin interaction with human civilization.
regions.notes: Wind-haunted ports suggest volatile weather; active smuggling routes; deepkin maintain presence in nearby waters.

regions.name: Bleached Wastes
regions.function: An exploration and cartography zone containing pre-human ruins that are awakening under the new stellar influence. A region of scholarly interest and cosmic mystery.
regions.notes: The pre-human ruins now glow with inner light and their inscriptions shimmer, suggesting they were built in anticipation of the current cosmic moment.

regions.name: The Iron Coast
regions.function: A coastal trading and smuggling hub where forbidden cargo moves and debts to the abyssal singers are tracked.
regions.notes: Home to Captain Vesta's operations. The Sapphire Deep is accessible from here. The region appears to be economically vibrant but spiritually burdened.

regions.name: Verdant Lands
regions.function: Ancient forests where circle-keepers dwell and tend to the covenant between earth and sky. A nexus of seasonal magic and territorial harmony now disrupted by the Stellar Rite.
regions.notes: The breaking of the Stellar Rite has caused seasonal magics to stutter, ley-lines to fail, and beasts to lose their territorial knowledge. The forest is slowly dying.

regions.name: The Scorched Reach
regions.function: A eastern region of Valdris most heavily affected by the Star-Rise, where villages vanish overnight and refugees flee westward.
regions.notes: The worst consequences of the cosmic rewrite manifest here. The boundary between mapped and unmapped void is dangerously thin.

regions.name: Bone Wastes
regions.function: A desolate borderland where ancient hermits maintain their vigil and magical census work continues despite instability.
regions.notes: Home to the Hermit Woman whose census working is collapsing due to the Stellar Rite.

regions.name: Burned Expanse
regions.function: A formerly inhabited region devastated by the Luminous Ascension; now a source of refugees carrying impossible memories and a staging ground for transformed beings drawn to the Sunken Cathedrals.
regions.notes: Reality is unstable; villages vanish; merchants transform into light.

regions.name: Charred Expanse
regions.function: A blighted region containing pre-human ruins that are now actively breaching their seals and singing. May be tied to the Luminous Remaking or an older cataclysm.
regions.notes: The pre-human structures beneath are compelling refugees and merchants eastward toward the Sunken Cathedrals. An ancient work seeks completion or witnesses.

regions.name: western provinces
regions.function: A remote territory where fragments of cosmic consciousness and memory-thieves operate outside the direct control of central authority.
regions.notes: The setting reinforces isolation and the thinness between cosmic and mortal realms.

regions.name: The Dominion
regions.function: A large realm or polity encompassing shadow-markets and trade routes for stolen memories and world-fragments.
regions.notes: The Dominion is actively collapsing. Vex's shadow-markets permeate it as the only stable economy. The Archivist operates within its power vacuum.

regions.name: Three Provinces (Hegemony)
regions.function: Administrative zones of the Hegemony where assessors and ledger-keeping occurred. Sites of cascading ledger-singing and assessor transformation.
regions.notes: The exact location and identity of these three provinces is not specified in the stories. They represent the span of the Hegemony's institutional reach.

regions.name: Scorched Lands
regions.function: A region where cosmic catastrophe and the Obsidian Tower's emergence create a convergence point for refugees, Overseers, and consciousness-seekers to negotiate with reality's reshaping.
regions.notes: Geography is unstable due to the Obsidian Tower's growth. Multiple factions converge here to decide the fate of the world.

regions.name: deepkin realms
regions.function: A realm or collection of realms sealed behind a dimensional boundary, calling to the material world as the boundary seal fractures.
regions.notes: The deepkin realms are adjacent to the abyss and separated from the surface kingdoms by the Tear of the Drowned God. As Meren's curse cracks, the realms call to her.

regions.name: Sunscoured Wastes
regions.function: A blighted land affected by the Luminous Ascension where material value dissolves into starlight; home to Skarath's hoard.
regions.notes: The Ascension has fundamentally altered this region's nature. Gold and solid matter dissolve here, making it uninhabitable for traditional hoarders.

regions.name: Wanderer's Location
regions.function: The epicenter of factional competition after the Wanderer's declarative statement. Multiple factions race toward this location.
regions.notes: The Wanderer's location is not specified but is contested ground after its revelation.

substances.name: stolen shadows
substances.properties: Unknown magical properties; untraced and untraceable; can be stored in clay jars; some are inhabited by malevolent entities.
substances.use: Traded in night markets as high-value illicit goods. Can be weaponized against their original owners if inhabited by malevolent forces.
substances.notes: Vex stole a fortune of these from a dying sorcerer's vault. They are valuable enough to attract multiple buyers in a single night. Some circulate the markets with hidden agendas.

substances.name: Malachar's Gold
substances.properties: Stamped with a skeletal crown. Carries autonomous supernatural debt. Compels holder into servitude.
substances.use: Intended as currency or treasure, but functions as a curse-delivery mechanism. Cannot be safely used as currency due to debt activation.
substances.notes: The gold itself is real and valuable, but the curse outweighs its worth. The curse activates regardless of the holder's knowledge or intent.

substances.name: septic rot
substances.properties: A manifestation of plague that blackens the skin of victims. Caused by the Rot-King. Spreads through a village rapidly.
substances.use: A curse or plague weapon; not used intentionally by mortals.
substances.notes: Lifted from the Drowned Hollows by Kess's bargain. The mechanism of transmission or cure is unknown.

substances.name: Restoration Honey
substances.tagline: An elixir that unmakes death itself
substances.description: A vial of golden liquid crafted by the Emperor's alchemist that possesses the power to resurrect the dead and bind them to the living world through alchemy. The magic requires a steep price: the severing of a vital connection between the resurrected and the world of the living, typically the voice or name of the one who performs the ritual. When consumed, it resurrects corpses in an undead state, sustained by its alchemical force rather than true life.
substances.status: Known to exist; dangerous and unpredictable

substances.name: salt-foam
substances.properties: Byproduct of oath-thing dissolution when brought to deepkin; appears to be the final state of consumed oaths.
substances.use: Indicator of oath settlement; ritual remainder; oceanic magic residue
substances.notes: Forms when oath-things are sung to dissolution by deepkin; may have magical properties unknown.

substances.name: Shadow-Fragments
substances.properties: Appears to be fragments of shadow with memory-retentive properties; can be traded as forbidden cargo
substances.use: Contraband; possibly related to memory magic or binding rituals
substances.notes: Vesta smuggles these as part of her forbidden cargo trade. Their exact properties and origin remain unclear.

substances.name: New Starlight
substances.properties: The light of the new stars created by the Celestial Ascension. Makes previously unreadable texts legible. Causes corpses to resonate and awaken. Transforms some beings into living light.
substances.use: Illuminates hidden knowledge; causes transformation and awakening effects; resonates with magic and the dead.
substances.notes: Appears to be the direct manifestation of the Celestial Ascension's reality-altering magic in the physical world.

substances.name: Stolen Memories
substances.properties: Fragments of Zareth's memories and experiences; visions of cosmic working and sacrifice; impressions of stars dying and being reborn; experiences that don't belong to the reader.
substances.use: Encoded into the Sovereign's ledger; imparted upon reading; cannot be unlearned; alters perception and identity of those exposed.
substances.notes: May be gift or poison; unclear if intentional encoding by Zareth or accidental consequence of the Ascension; dangerous knowledge.

substances.name: Amber
substances.properties: Used as wealth and payment. The Hermitage of Keth'morian offers amber as payment for thieving commissions.
substances.use: Currency and trade good.
substances.notes: Often paired with 'silence' as payment—suggesting both material and discretionary compensation.

substances.name: Living light
substances.properties: Transforms flesh into a hybrid state of physical and luminous matter; gradually dissolves human memory and identity; compels affected individuals toward specific locations.
substances.use: A byproduct of the Luminous Ascension; transforms merchants and refugees against their will.
substances.notes: Iskar the Spice-Merchant is a living example of this substance's effects.

substances.name: Harmonic Frequency
substances.properties: A frequency of resonance that links the Trench, Sunken Cathedrals, Abyss-gate, and Singing Caverns. Emitted by pre-human sites and used to conduct their working across vast distances and possibly across time.
substances.use: Used by pre-human civilization to orchestrate their working. Can be sensed, conducted, and possibly amplified through seals and other artifacts.
